PMC10999549.diagnosis: Gallbladder torsion (gallbladder volvulus) causing ischemic/hemorrhagic acute cholecystitis
rationale_summary: Acute epigastric/RLQ pain with vomiting and positive Murphy's sign but no jaundice and normal hepatic/biliary labs
Ultrasound: enlarged gallbladder with wall thickening and pericholecystic fluid/fat stranding without gallstones

Repeat ultrasound: absent gallbladder wall vascular flow plus cone-shaped hypoechoic structure at the neck and a gallbladder that is displaced from its fossa and mobile with repositioning (suggesting twisted pedicle)

CT: fundus deviated left of the gallbladder bed with poorly enhancing irregular wall, intraluminal hyperdensity consistent with hemorrhage, and focal hepatic perfusion abnormalities-supporting torsion with ischemia/infarction

PMC11535749.diagnosis: SBP-101-associated toxic maculopathy (drug-induced outer retinal/RPE degeneration)
rationale_summary: Normal baseline ophthalmic exam/OCT before SBP-101, with vision decline beginning after the second monthly SBP-101 cycle

Bilateral progressive macular pigmentary changes with punctate hypo-/hyperautofluorescence, ellipsoid zone disruption, and hyperreflective sub-RPE material on OCT evolving to RPE/outer retinal atrophy with choroidal thinning
ERG showing relatively preserved rod responses with reduced 30-Hz flicker amplitudes/implicit time changes consistent with predominantly cone/macular dysfunction
Paraneoplastic retinopathy workup negative (anti-recoverin and anti-enolase within normal limits), supporting drug toxicity rather than autoimmune retinopathy

PMC11821356.diagnosis: Adult-onset PFAPA syndrome (Periodic Fever, Aphthous stomatitis, Pharyngitis, Adenitis)
rationale_summary: Recurrent, stereotyped febrile episodes every 6-8 weeks for 5 years with complete wellness between attacks and predictable onset
Each episode characterized by exudative pharyngitis, sore throat/dysphagia, and painful cervical/submandibular lymphadenopathy
Inflammatory markers elevated during attacks (CRP, serum amyloid A) with repeatedly negative infectious workup and no response to antibiotics
Lymph node biopsy showed reactive hyperplasia and bone marrow/cytogenetics were unremarkable, arguing against malignancy or chronic infection

PMC10216786.diagnosis: Hyperventilation-induced acute hypophosphatemic paralysis (respiratory alkalosis-related intracellular phosphate shift), with rhabdomyolysis
rationale_summary: Both episodes occurred with acute hyperventilation/hypocapnia and documented respiratory alkalosis (pH 7.57, PaCO2 19.2)
Severe hypophosphatemia was present during attacks (as low as 0.2 mmol/L) with acute tetraparesis and tetany/positive Trousseau's sign
Electrophysiology (nerve conduction studies/myography) and brain/spine MRI were normal, supporting a metabolic rather than structural/neuromuscular junction cause
Hypokalemic periodic paralysis and myasthenia gravis workups were negative; potassium, calcium, magnesium, and thyroid tests were normal

No renal phosphate wasting on urinalysis, favoring redistribution hypophosphatemia triggered by hyperventilation; second episode showed marked CK elevation consistent with hypophosphatemia-associated rhabdomyolysis

PMC7325212.diagnosis: HINT1-associated axonal motor neuropathy with neuromyotonia (autosomal recessive HINT1 neuropathy)
rationale_summary: Progressive, length-dependent distal lower-limb weakness with bilateral foot drop and absent knee/ankle reflexes
NCS shows reduced peroneal/tibial CMAP amplitudes with normal velocities/latencies and normal sensory studies, supporting a predominantly motor axonal neuropathy
Needle EMG shows chronic denervation (fibrillations/positive sharp waves, long-duration polyphasic MUPs with reduced recruitment) plus prominent myotonic discharges

PMC6815408.diagnosis: Relapsing-remitting multiple sclerosis (demyelinating brainstem syndrome involving bilateral PPRF)
rationale_summary: Adolescent with recurrent neurologic episodes separated in time (hand paresthesia 1 year prior; progressive feet-to-trunk sensory symptoms 2 months prior; acute gaze palsy now), suggesting relapsing disease
MRI shows multifocal demyelinating-appearing lesions in typical MS locations (periventricular and subcortical brain lesions) plus spinal cord lesions at C7 and T4 (dissemination in space)
Active inflammation supported by gadolinium enhancement of the T4 spinal lesion (dissemination in time)
Acute bilateral horizontal gaze palsy localizes to pontine gaze centers; MRI shows bilateral PPRF hyperintensities at pontine level consistent with demyelinating brainstem involvement
Anti-aquaporin-4 and anti-MOG antibodies negative, making NMOSD/MOGAD less likely in this presentation

PMC3423660.diagnosis: Disseminated Bartonella henselae infection (atypical cat-scratch disease)
rationale_summary: Initial presentation with painful left axillary lymphadenopathy and low-grade fever

Systemic features developed (chronic conjunctivitis, purpuric/ulcerative skin lesions, persistent fevers) without evidence of lymphoproliferation
Bartonella henselae serology was positive (titer 1:128), supporting Bartonella-associated necrotizing lymphadenitis/systemic disease

PMC7993351.diagnosis: Classic (non-exertional) heat stroke precipitated by impaired thermoregulation from anticholinergic medications
rationale_summary: Found unresponsive during a heatwave in a non-air-conditioned home with rectal temperature 42°C
Hypotension (84/42 mmHg) and altered mental status (responsive only to painful stimuli) consistent with heat stroke
Warm, dry skin and dry mucous membranes suggest impaired sweating/dehydration
Multiple anticholinergic/antimuscarinic drugs (amitriptyline, benztropine, cyclobenzaprine) that can reduce sweating and increase heat injury risk
No rigidity or hyperreflexia and normal creatine phosphokinase, arguing against neuroleptic malignant syndrome or serotonin syndrome

PMC6601241.diagnosis: Myocardial (cardiac) metastasis from recurrent squamous cell carcinoma of the tongue causing a pseudo-ST-elevation myocardial infarction pattern
rationale_summary: History of tongue squamous cell carcinoma treated 16 months prior, now with frailty/weight loss and new metastatic-appearing findings (tender right supraclavicular lymphadenopathy, progression of lung cavitary lesion, new right clavicular lesion)

PMC11294736.diagnosis: Sulfonylurea-induced hypoglycemia from ingestion of a counterfeit benzodiazepine pill (e.g., glyburide-contaminated "diazepam")
rationale_summary: Recurrent severe hypoglycemia (glucose 15-44 mg/dL) requiring repeated IV dextrose
Inappropriately high insulin (47.4 mIU/mL) and elevated C-peptide (5.4 ng/mL) during hypoglycemia indicates endogenous insulin secretion, not exogenous insulin
No history of diabetes or prescribed hypoglycemics; onset followed ingestion of two "light blue" pills obtained from a friend and presumed to be diazepam
Insulin antibodies, cortisol, and thyroid studies were normal, making autoimmune or endocrine deficiency causes less likely

PMC4689918.diagnosis: Embryonal rhabdomyosarcoma with prominent intracytoplasmic lipid vacuolation (lipoblast-like/rhabdomyoblastic fatty change)
rationale_summary: 16-year-old with large intramuscular thigh tumor; initial FNA showed spindle/ovoid cells with eosinophilic cytoplasm and desmin/myogenin positivity, supporting rhabdomyosarcoma
FOXO1 rearrangement negative, arguing against alveolar rhabdomyosarcoma
Excision showed sheets of rhabdomyoblastic round cells; many cells had adipocyte/lipoblast-like vacuoles that were Oil Red O and adipophilin positive (true lipid) and PAS negative
Vacuolated cells retained myogenic differentiation (desmin positive) rather than true adipocytic neoplasm, supporting embryonal rhabdomyosarcoma with lipidized rhabdomyoblasts

PMC7676809.diagnosis: Right distal ureterolithiasis (ureteral stone mimicking appendicitis)

Ultrasound shows a hyperechoic circular focus with posterior acoustic shadowing consistent with a calculus

The calculus is within a hypoechoic, non-peristalsing, non-compressible tubular structure adjacent to the iliac vessels with thin walls and no intestinal wall layering, consistent with ureter rather than bowel/appendix
Absence of surrounding fat stranding, free fluid, or hyperemia further argues against appendicitis

PMC8557694.diagnosis: Primary CNS lymphoma (diffuse large B‑cell lymphoma), lymphomatosis cerebri-type presentation
rationale_summary: Progressive cerebellar/brainstem-type symptoms with diffuse deep white-matter T2/FLAIR abnormalities that later developed corpus callosum contrast enhancement
CSF on two occasions showed atypical lymphoid cells and an abnormal mature B‑cell population despite normal cell count/protein, supporting a CNS B‑cell neoplastic process
Negative infectious and paraneoplastic testing and no systemic disease on PET/ophthalmologic exam favor a primary CNS process

Stereotactic biopsy showing gliosis with mixed perivascular/parenchymal inflammation can occur with steroid-treated/undersampled primary CNS lymphoma, after which symptoms and imaging continued to worsen despite autoimmune-directed therapy

PMC7294351.diagnosis: Acute disseminated encephalomyelitis (ADEM) associated with HIV infection
rationale_summary: Subacute febrile encephalopathy with seizures, hallucinations, and multifocal neurologic deficits (arm paresis, paraparesis, gait disturbance)

Opportunistic focal CNS infections/PCNSL are less likely given preserved CD4 count (669 cells/μL) and negative toxoplasma IgG

Bone marrow had low plasma cell burden (3%) and no other systemic organ findings reported, favoring localized pulmonary amyloidosis over systemic disease

PMC4637487.diagnosis: AL (light-chain) amyloidosis from IgG-kappa plasma cell dyscrasia causing bilateral phrenic neuropathy with diaphragmatic weakness
rationale_summary: Known IgG-kappa multiple myeloma with markedly rising kappa free light chains and extreme K/L ratio
Progressive dyspnea with restrictive PFTs and hypercapnic respiratory failure (pCO2 60 mmHg) suggests ventilatory muscle weakness rather than primary lung disease

Weight loss and dysphagia with multi-organ involvement risk in light-chain disease supports systemic AL amyloidosis/neuropathy as the unifying cause

PMC5054479.diagnosis: Congenital insensitivity to pain with anhidrosis (HSAN type IV, NTRK1-related) complicated by secondary AA amyloidosis causing nephrotic syndrome
rationale_summary: Infancy-onset recurrent fevers with absence of sweating (anhidrosis)
Severe self-mutilation beginning with teething (biting fingers/toes, tongue/skin injuries) consistent with insensitivity to pain
Repeated unrecognized trauma with fractures, recurrent joint dislocations, chronic ulcers and osteomyelitis with polymicrobial infection
Reported Lesch-Nyhan but uric acid is normal, arguing against hyperuricemic self-injury
Later massive proteinuria (6.29 g/24 h), hypoalbuminemia and ascites compatible with nephrotic syndrome likely secondary to chronic inflammation/infection (AA amyloidosis)

PMC11481506.diagnosis: Hereditary angioedema presenting as an abdominal (visceral) attack
rationale_summary: Severe acute abdominal pain with rebound and positive Murphy's sign but afebrile with normal procalcitonin and C-reactive protein, arguing against infectious cholecystitis/peritonitis
CT showed diffuse third-spacing (pleural/pericardial/abdominal fluid) with bowel wall edema and only mild gallbladder wall swelling, consistent with visceral edema/serosal effusions
Emergency laparotomy found no perforation, gallbladder enlargement, or cholangitis, but large-volume ascites (~1000 mL) and bowel wall edema, supporting angioedema as the cause of the acute abdomen

PMC3961933.diagnosis: Central cemento-ossifying fibroma (giant ossifying/cementifying fibroma) of the mandible
rationale_summary: 6-year, painless, slowly enlarging mandibular swelling causing marked facial asymmetry and chewing difficulty
Imaging shows a well-defined, expansile mixed radiolucent-radiopaque lesion with "cotton-wool" calcifications and concentric/ball-like growth, with bowing but no erosion of the inferior border

Bicortical expansion with intact mucosa and no cervical lymphadenopathy supports a benign, well-circumscribed fibro-osseous neoplasm rather than fibrous dysplasia (typically ill-defined) or inflammatory disease
Normal calcium, phosphorus, and alkaline phosphatase levels argue against systemic metabolic bone disease

PMC10499291.diagnosis: Gorham-Stout disease (idiopathic massive osteolysis) of the left mandible
rationale_summary: Serial panoramic radiographs over 18 months show slowly progressive, patchy-to-extensive osteolysis starting at the left mandibular condyle and extending through the angle toward the body, with tooth mobility, pain, and reduced mouth opening
Cone-beam CT with 3D reconstruction confirms marked bone loss confined to the left hemi-mandible
Whole-body PET shows no additional lytic lesions, supporting a localized idiopathic osteolysis rather than multifocal/systemic disease
Targeted workup was performed to exclude other causes of mandibular lysis (e.g., rheumatoid arthritis, multiple myeloma, eosinophilic granuloma, other systemic diseases)

PMC11825525.diagnosis: Disseminated (reactivated) tuberculosis due to anti‑TNF (adalimumab) immunosuppression
rationale_summary: On adalimumab for rheumatoid arthritis, a major risk factor for reactivation/disseminated TB
Constitutional symptoms (weight loss, night sweats), splenomegaly, and PET‑CT hypermetabolic lesions in spleen/liver with pulmonary nodules and lymphadenopathy consistent with disseminated infection
Biopsies from spleen and liver showed granulomatous inflammation (non‑necrotizing granulomas can occur in TB, especially with immunosuppression)
Repeat interferon‑gamma release assay (T‑SPOT.TB) became positive despite prior negative screening

Clinical improvement on corticosteroids with relapse when tapered and subsequent severe febrile pulmonary deterioration after restarting steroids/adalimumab supports an unrecognized underlying TB unmasked/worsened by immunosuppression

PMC6544423.diagnosis: Bilateral critical subclavian artery stenosis/occlusion causing coronary-subclavian steal with compromised LIMA/RIMA bypass graft flow (pseudohypotension in both arms leading to myocardial ischemia, pulmonary edema, and shock)
rationale_summary: Prior CABG used both LIMA and RIMA, making coronary perfusion dependent on subclavian inflow
Known bilateral subclavian artery stenosis (50% on MR angiography two years earlier) with progressive decline in arm blood pressures over months
Severe diffuse ischemia on ECG, positive troponin/CPK rise, and new LV systolic dysfunction with acute pulmonary edema

Persistent very low arm blood pressures/difficult measurement while femoral pulses remained easily palpable, supporting upper-extremity arterial obstruction/pseudohypotension rather than systemic circulatory collapse

Multisystem involvement typical of scrub typhus: hypotensive shock, hypoxemic pneumonia/pulmonary edema with pleural effusion, jaundice with transaminitis/cholestasis, and acute kidney injury
Extensive testing for other common febrile causes (dengue, leptospirosis, brucellosis, malaria, COVID-19, TB, HIV) was negative
Neuroimaging shows acute right frontal parenchymal hemorrhage, consistent with scrub-typhus-associated vasculitic/CNS complication in severe disease

PMC10551103.diagnosis: Areca nut poisoning causing acute non-immune intravascular hemolysis (hemoglobinuria) with acute hepatic injury
rationale_summary: Abrupt clinical deterioration immediately after receiving two tablespoons of areca nut powder (pallor, cold extremities, gross hematuria, seizures).
Marked LDH elevation (3570 U/L) with negative direct/indirect Coombs and sufficient G6PD activity supports non-immune hemolysis as the source of gross hematuria/hemoglobinuria.
Severe acute hepatocellular injury (AST 2670 U/L, ALT 970 U/L) with INR 1.76 and negative viral studies supports toxin-related hepatitis rather than infection.

PMC8577092.diagnosis: Cerebral salt-wasting syndrome (renal salt wasting) following intracerebral hemorrhage
rationale_summary: Recent intracerebral hemorrhage followed by several days of marked polyuria and 6-kg weight loss with clinical hypovolemia (dry mucosa, skin-fold dehydration, collapsible IVC) progressing to shock
Hypotension and lactic acidosis improved with aggressive isotonic saline, supporting volume-depletion physiology rather than cardiogenic/obstructive cause (normal echo, no tamponade)

Hyponatremia with low plasma osmolality plus inappropriately high urine sodium (62 mmol/L) and urine chloride (64 mmol/L) despite hypovolemia indicates renal salt loss rather than extrarenal losses/SIADH
Associated hypokalemia, hypochloremia, and metabolic alkalosis consistent with renal electrolyte wasting in the setting of volume depletion

PMC11232757.diagnosis: Generalized odontodysplasia ("ghost teeth") with associated hypodontia
rationale_summary: Multiple teeth show enamel chipping with dentin exposure and generalized enamel opacities
Radiographs show marked radiolucency involving enamel and dentin (and in maxillary incisors extending to pulp), consistent with hypomineralized "ghost" tooth structure
Maxillary incisors have widened periodontal ligament spaces and are nonvital on pulp testing, fitting odontodysplasia-associated pulpal involvement/nonvitality
Congenital absence of multiple mandibular anterior teeth supports a developmental odontogenic anomaly with hypodontia

rationale_summary: Cheyne-Stokes respiration with cyclic tachycardia/bradycardia and transient complete AV block began a few hours after loading ticagrelor (with aspirin) for PCI

PMC6006492.diagnosis: Junctional ectopic tachycardia (incessant automatic junctional tachycardia)
rationale_summary: Very rapid narrow-complex tachycardia (250-280 bpm) unresponsive to vagal maneuvers and escalating IV adenosine
Repeated electrical cardioversions failed to terminate the rhythm, supporting a non-reentrant automatic focus
Only transient/partial rate control with multiple antiarrhythmics; rate slowed with cooling
When slowed to ~130 bpm, ECG showed atrioventricular dissociation, consistent with a junctional tachycardia mechanism

PMC11573733.diagnosis: Cardiac amyloidosis causing restrictive cardiomyopathy (most consistent with AL amyloidosis)
rationale_summary: Progressive right- and left-sided heart failure symptoms with elevated JVP, hepatomegaly, ascites, pleural effusion, and edema despite diuretics/ACE inhibitor intolerance (low BP).
ECG with low-voltage limb leads and pseudoinfarction pattern despite marked ventricular wall thickening on echo.

Echocardiography shows biatrial enlargement, marked concentric biventricular hypertrophy with interatrial septal thickening, granular "sparkling" myocardium, thickened valves, small pericardial effusion, and restrictive diastolic filling with preserved LVEF-classic for infiltrative amyloid cardiomyopathy.
Troponin elevation without regional wall motion abnormalities supports myocardial infiltration/injury rather than acute coronary syndrome.

PMC8536869.diagnosis: Pacemaker lead dislodgement due to Twiddler syndrome (device/lead manipulation causing extracardiac stimulation and loss of capture)
rationale_summary: 3 months after dual-lead pacemaker implantation, both leads were displaced on chest radiograph (atrial lead to SVC, ventricular lead in right atrium)

Rhythmic abdominal wall contractions at ~60 bpm resolved when the pacemaker was deactivated, indicating pacer-driven extracardiac muscle stimulation
Risk factors present for Twiddler syndrome: elderly, overweight, mild cognitive impairment, delayed post-implant presentation

PMC9334930.diagnosis: Central-variant posterior reversible encephalopathy syndrome (hypertensive encephalopathy with bilateral basal ganglia involvement)
rationale_summary: Developed persistent, escalating severe systolic hypertension (up to 200 mm Hg) despite maximal antihypertensives, in the setting of end-stage renal failure requiring continuous hemodialysis

Initial post-arrest imaging lacked basal ganglia abnormalities, supporting a later hypertension-related process rather than primary hypoxic-ischemic basal ganglia injury

PMC8840641.diagnosis: SMARCA4-deficient thoracic sarcoma (SMARCA4-deficient undifferentiated tumor of the thorax)
rationale_summary: Large aggressive thoracic mass extending from lung to anterior mediastinum in a heavy smoker with rapid fatal course
Histology with sheets of monotonous epithelial-like cells, rhabdoid-like cells, frequent mitoses, and necrosis
Immunophenotype: loss of SMARCA4 and SMARCA2 with positivity for SOX2 and CD34; keratin (AE1/AE3) positive but lacks lung lineage markers (TTF-1) and squamous markers (p40)
Molecular testing negative for typical lung adenocarcinoma drivers (EGFR, ALK, ROS1), supporting a SMARCA4-deficient thoracic tumor rather than conventional NSCLC

PMC4323579.diagnosis: Neutrophilic dermatosis of the hands (localized Sweet syndrome)
rationale_summary: Febrile patient with acute, painful, well-demarcated violaceous plaques on multiple digits that rapidly extended to nonadjacent fingers
Marked inflammatory markers (WBC 19.8×10^9/L, CRP 121 mg/L) with negative wound cultures and no response to broad antimicrobials
Histology showed acute inflammatory infiltrate with reactive changes and no neoplastic cells, supporting an inflammatory (neutrophilic) dermatosis rather than infection
Strong association with hematologic malignancy in this patient (newly diagnosed acute myeloid leukemia)

Severe disorientation, major thought block, incoherent/bizarre speech, and catatonia-like motor findings (increased tone, spastic movements with preserved withdrawal to pain) suggest an organic encephalitic process rather than primary panic disorder.
Normal MRI brain and largely unremarkable CSF studies do not exclude autoimmune encephalitis; early anti-NMDA cases can have normal routine imaging/CSF.
Elevated prolactin supports possible seizure activity/limbic involvement, which can accompany anti-NMDA receptor encephalitis presenting with prominent psychiatric symptoms.

PMC11018330.diagnosis: Durvalumab-associated acute zonal occult outer retinopathy (AZOOR-like immune-mediated outer retinopathy)
rationale_summary: New 2-month temporal C-shaped scotoma after five cycles of durvalumab for small-cell lung cancer

Fundus largely unremarkable in the symptomatic left eye but fundus autofluorescence shows broad C-shaped hypoautofluorescent lesions with hyperautofluorescent margins in both eyes (outer retinal/RPE zone involvement)
OCT shows peripapillary ellipsoid zone loss in the left eye corresponding to the visual field defect (outer retinal photoreceptor damage)
Fluorescein angiography shows normal vascular perfusion, arguing against ischemic/vascular causes
Brain MRI metastasis did not anatomically explain the visual field defect, supporting a retinal origin

PMC9022340.diagnosis: Diffuse large B-cell lymphoma with extensive hepatic involvement (stage IV extranodal lymphoma) associated with antibody-negative Graves-type thyrotoxicosis
rationale_summary: Markedly elevated LDH (3054 U/L) with anemia (Hgb 8.1 g/dL) and hepatomegaly with tender enlargement

18F-FDG PET-CT shows multiple large FDG-avid liver masses (SUVmax 13.5) plus mesenteric lymphadenopathy and additional FDG-avid sites (gastric antrum uptake, pulmonary infiltrates), consistent with disseminated aggressive lymphoma

Concurrent thyrotoxicosis (elevated total/free T4 with suppressed TSH) with diffusely increased uptake on Tc-99m pertechnetate scan supports Graves-type hyperthyroidism despite negative TSI/TRAb, which can coexist as a paraneoplastic/associated autoimmune phenomenon

Cardiopulmonary findings (pulmonary hypertension, right-sided dilation, tricuspid regurgitation) are compatible with severe thyrotoxicosis contributing to high-output/right-heart strain in the setting of an ASD

PMC4374329.diagnosis: Orofacial granulomatosis (cheilitis granulomatosa with granulomatous gingivitis)
rationale_summary: 2-year, painless, gradually progressive diffuse swelling of both lips with fissuring and cobblestone appearance
Concomitant diffuse gingival enlargement (13-23) that is red, smooth/shiny with bleeding on probing and false pockets but no attachment loss
Panoramic radiograph shows no alveolar bone loss, supporting inflammatory enlargement rather than periodontitis
Normal ACE level, unremarkable chest radiograph, and negative Mantoux test argue against sarcoidosis and tuberculosis as causes of granulomatous orofacial swelling
No history of allergy or atopy, making allergic angioedema less likely in a chronic, persistent presentation

PMC6421145.diagnosis: Mid-ventricular obstructive hypertrophic cardiomyopathy with left-ventricular apical aneurysm and intraventricular thrombus
rationale_summary: Transthoracic echocardiography showed asymmetric left ventricular hypertrophy with midventricular high-velocity flow (2.00 m/s systole, 3.67 m/s diastole) and paradoxical early-diastolic jet from apex to base, consistent with midventricular obstruction

Frequent PVCs and nonsustained ventricular tachycardia are compatible with hypertrophic cardiomyopathy complicated by apical aneurysm

PMC6557017.diagnosis: Acute myeloid leukemia with mutated NPM1 (NPM1-mutated myeloid neoplasm presenting with CMML-like features)
rationale_summary: Progressive leukocytosis with neutrophilia/monocytosis plus anemia and thrombocytopenia
Bone marrow markedly hypercellular with myeloid/myelomonocytic hyperplasia, trilineage dysplasia, and increased blasts (4% initially; persistent 6% peripheral blood blasts later)
Next-generation sequencing showed a pathogenic NPM1 mutation (with NRAS), supporting an NPM1-mutated AML spectrum even with lower blast percentages
CT demonstrated bulky multistation lymphadenopathy and hepatosplenomegaly, consistent with systemic/extramedullary leukemic involvement rather than isolated CMML

PMC10962233.diagnosis: Disseminated cryptococcosis with laryngeal involvement (laryngeal cryptococcoma)

PMC3657244.diagnosis: Lucio phenomenon (necrotizing vasculitis) complicating diffuse lepromatous leprosy
rationale_summary: Underlying diffuse lepromatous leprosy supported by facial infiltration with saddle-nose, shiny infiltrated back skin, symmetric non-tender peripheral nerve thickening with glove-and-stocking anesthesia, and slit-skin smear showing globi with BI 4+

Acute onset of painless hemorrhagic edema and hemorrhagic bullae on hands/feet/earlobes that rapidly rupture into deep irregular ulcers with angulated margins and surrounding purpura-classic for Lucio phenomenon in diffuse lepromatous leprosy
Lack of fever/arthralgia or tender nodules argues against erythema nodosum leprosum; negative cryoglobulins/RF/ANA and normal renal/liver tests make other vasculitides less likely

PMC8085510.diagnosis: Primary hepatic high-grade neuroendocrine carcinoma (grade 3) with markedly elevated AFP
rationale_summary: Core biopsy of the segment 4 liver mass showed malignant neuroendocrine morphology with synaptophysin positivity and Ki-67 ~30% (high-grade neuroendocrine carcinoma)

Very high AFP and AFP-L3% suggested hepatocellular differentiation clinically, but liver biopsy favored a neuroendocrine carcinoma rather than typical hepatocellular carcinoma, in a non-cirrhotic liver

PMC10725598.diagnosis: Sclerosing epithelioid fibrosarcoma (MUC4-positive sarcoma) with metastatic disease
rationale_summary: Liver biopsy showed an undifferentiated epithelioid neoplasm in nests/cords within a hyalinized sclerotic stroma, a classic pattern for sclerosing epithelioid fibrosarcoma
Tumor cells were MUC4-reactive with preserved INI1 and BRG1, supporting SEF/LGFMS-spectrum sarcoma
Broad epithelial markers were negative (pankeratin, CK7, CK20, MOC-31, claudin-4), arguing against carcinoma/mesothelioma despite lung mass-like consolidation
Negative melanocytic markers (HMB45, Melan-A, SOX10, S100) and vascular markers (ERG, CD31, CAMTA1) helped exclude melanoma and epithelioid vascular tumors
Diffuse lytic vertebral lesions and a hepatic lesion support widely metastatic behavior consistent with SEF

PMC7036447.diagnosis: Retinal neovascularization with vitreous hemorrhage secondary to chronic central serous chorioretinopathy (CSCR)
rationale_summary: Longstanding chronic CSCR with bilateral RPE/photoreceptor degeneration and gravitational tracks on fundus autofluorescence

Left eye showed a branched vascular proliferation arising from the inferior temporal arcade projecting into the vitreous with vitreous hemorrhage while peripheral retina and remaining vasculature were normal
OCTA confirmed a flow-positive vascular network protruding into the vitreous, supporting retinal (not subretinal) neovascularization
Common systemic/infectious causes of retinal neovascularization (diabetes, infection) were excluded by history and blood tests

Restrictive physiology with reduced DLCO on PFT supports an interstitial inflammatory lung process

PMC7701014.diagnosis: Lepromatous leprosy (Hansen disease) involving the auricular and nasal cartilage with upper-airway involvement
rationale_summary: Chronic bilateral swelling of cartilaginous ear helices and nasal bridge with overlying red-brown nodules suggests an infiltrative infectious/granulomatous process rather than idiopathic relapsing polychondritis

Lack of sustained response to methotrexate and mycophenolate for presumed relapsing polychondritis supports an alternative diagnosis such as Hansen disease

PMC9069601.diagnosis: Chronic Coxiella burnetii (Q fever) vascular graft infection causing immune-complex leukocytoclastic vasculitis and lupus-like immune-complex glomerulonephritis
rationale_summary: Two-year history of fevers/night sweats with very high ESR/CRP and anemia suggests chronic infection
History of femoral crossover vascular graft provides a potential occult infection source despite negative routine blood cultures and unrevealing initial imaging/echo
Palpable purpura with skin biopsy showing leukocytoclastic vasculitis with IgM and C3 deposition supports immune-complex small-vessel vasculitis

Glomerular hematuria (acanthocytes) and renal biopsy showing mesangial hypercellularity with mesangial electron-dense deposits and C3/IgM/C1q staining is consistent with infection-related immune-complex GN that can mimic lupus
Serologies were atypical for lupus (only weak ANA, dsDNA negative, complements normal) and disease repeatedly relapsed when prednisone was tapered, prompting reconsideration of autoimmune diagnosis

PMC4310044.diagnosis: Neuroglial (glioependymal/neuroepithelial) cyst of the right middle cerebellar peduncle
rationale_summary: Well-defined lobulated intra-axial cystic lesion with multiple thin septations that is uniformly CSF-isointense on all MRI sequences

Single-voxel MR spectroscopy shows unremarkable metabolite concentrations and normal ratios, supporting a benign non-neoplastic cyst
Location in the cerebellar peduncle adjacent to the fourth ventricle with mild mass effect/edema fits a neuroepithelial (glioependymal) cyst presentation

PMC8962241.diagnosis: Systemic arterial cerebral air embolism secondary to left hilar lung cancer invading the left atrium/bronchus (bronchopulmonary venous/atrial fistula), complicated by Staphylococcus aureus bacteremia
rationale_summary: Acute focal neurologic deficits (aphasia, left weakness, neglect) on day 2
Head CT showed multiple small intracranial low-density areas interpreted as air emboli
Brain MRI showed cortical/subcortical diffusion abnormalities compatible with embolic ischemia
Chest CT and echocardiography demonstrated tumor invasion into the left atrium and left main bronchus, providing a pathway for systemic arterial air entry
Concurrent Staphylococcus aureus bacteremia and low-density areas within/around the tumor suggest infected/necrotic tumor facilitating fistula and embolization

PMC3608321.diagnosis: Ectopic ACTH syndrome (occult ectopic ACTH-secreting neuroendocrine tumor) causing ACTH-dependent Cushing syndrome
rationale_summary: Clinical Cushingoid features with markedly elevated urinary free cortisol and loss of diurnal cortisol rhythm
Plasma ACTH persistently inappropriately elevated (30-45 ng/L) indicating ACTH-dependent hypercortisolism
No cortisol suppression with low- or high-dose dexamethasone argues against pituitary-dependent Cushing disease
Pituitary MRI negative and inferior petrosal sinus sampling after CRH shows no central-to-peripheral ACTH gradient and no ACTH rise, supporting an ectopic ACTH source
Workup for ectopic source is unrevealing (normal catecholamines/5-HIAA, negative 111In-pentetreotide scan), consistent with an occult ectopic ACTH-producing tumor

PMC8186929.diagnosis: Anomalous right coronary artery arising from the pulmonary artery (ARCAPA) causing coronary steal with acute biventricular failure/cardiogenic shock
rationale_summary: Coronary angiography showed markedly ectatic left main/LAD/LCx without obstructive stenoses, arguing against flow-limiting CAD as the cause of shock/troponin rise
The RCA ostium could not be engaged from the aorta, suggesting an anomalous RCA origin
Left coronary contrast demonstrated retrograde collateral filling of the RCA, consistent with an RCA not originating from the aorta
Further review indicated the RCA was emptying into the pulmonary artery/right ventricular outflow tract, diagnostic of ARCAPA physiology (left-to-right shunt/coronary steal)

Presentation with atrial fibrillation, rapid hemodynamic deterioration, biventricular systolic dysfunction, and marked troponin elevation is compatible with ischemia and cardiogenic shock from coronary steal in ARCAPA

PMC10191493.diagnosis: Isolated gastroduodenal Crohn disease causing a stricturing duodenal obstruction
rationale_summary: Subacute weight loss, abdominal pain, bilious vomiting with elevated inflammatory markers and thrombocytosis suggests an inflammatory bowel process
Imaging showed marked dilation of the stomach and proximal duodenum with decompressed distal duodenum, localizing a mechanical obstruction to the second portion of the duodenum

Endoscopy demonstrated complete obstruction at the second portion of the duodenum, and biopsies proximal to the stricture showed chronic gastritis and active duodenitis without infection or malignancy, consistent with inflammatory stricturing disease

PMC10901261.diagnosis: Severe secondary (functional) mitral regurgitation complicating end-stage ischaemic cardiomyopathy with low-output cardiogenic shock
rationale_summary: Known ischaemic cardiomyopathy with NYHA class IV symptoms and INTERMACS III status, unable to wean from dobutamine with end-organ dysfunction (renal, hepatic, respiratory) indicating low output

Transoesophageal echocardiography shows markedly dilated LV (EDD 72 mm) with EF ~20% and severe central MR from annular dilatation and leaflet tethering (Carpentier I and IIIb), consistent with secondary MR

Quantification supports severe/disproportionate MR (EROA 80 mm², regurgitant volume 76 mL) with haemodynamic consequences (giant LA V waves to 65 mmHg, pulmonary artery systolic pressure 60 mmHg, pulmonary oedema on chest radiograph)

PMC6257925.diagnosis: Iris tissue incarceration/herniation into the trabecular meshwork (post-trabeculotomy/trabeculectomy)
rationale_summary: Left eye has prior angle surgery (trabeculotomy/trabeculectomy) and multiple cyclophotocoagulation sessions
Gonioscopy shows dark brown raised mounds within the trabecular meshwork resembling iris tissue in multiple quadrants
Anterior segment OCT confirms elevated tissue within an otherwise open angle, without iris or ciliary body mass
Lesions remained unchanged over 6 months and the eye is quiet with no signs of pigment dispersion or inflammation

PMC2934912.diagnosis: Brucellosis (likely Brucella melitensis) with cholestatic hepatitis and SIADH-related hyponatremia
rationale_summary: Sheep farmer who consumes nonpasteurized cheese (classic exposure for brucellosis)
Two weeks of nocturnal fever and arthralgia with hepatomegaly and mild splenomegaly
Marked cholestatic liver enzyme pattern (alkaline phosphatase 2340 U/L, γ-GGT 597 U/L) with direct hyperbilirubinemia and no biliary dilatation on imaging

Hyponatremia with low plasma osmolality and inappropriately elevated urine osmolality and urine sodium in an euvolemic patient with normal thyroid/adrenal tests (consistent with SIADH occurring with systemic infection)
Negative EBV/CMV/hepatitis serologies; mild pericardial effusion consistent with systemic infectious involvement

PMC5807253.diagnosis: Metformin-associated lactic acidosis (MALA) precipitated by acute kidney injury and shock from severe dehydration
rationale_summary: On metformin 1000 mg twice daily with abrupt severe acute kidney injury (creatinine 8.07 mg/dL from 0.86) allowing metformin accumulation
Profound high-anion gap metabolic acidosis (pH 6.57, HCO3 2, anion gap 30) with marked hyperlactatemia (lactate 16.3 mmol/L)
Presentation with severe diarrhea/vomiting, hypotension requiring vasopressors, and cardiac arrest consistent with shock precipitating/worsening lactic acidosis in a metformin-treated patient

PMC5896219.diagnosis: Acute compartment syndrome of the right forearm/hand precipitated by acute crystal arthritis (gout with tophaceous tenosynovitis)
rationale_summary: Progressive forearm/hand pain and swelling with tense compartments, severe pain on passive stretch, sensory changes, and decreased capillary refill
Elevated compartment pressures (20-24 mm Hg volar forearm; 45-48 mm Hg carpal tunnel/palm) prompting urgent fasciotomy
Joint aspirates showed monosodium urate crystals (elbow) and calcium pyrophosphate crystals (wrist) with no bacterial growth
Intraoperative finding of small tophi in flexor tendon synovial sheaths and no pus/necrosis supports crystal-related inflammatory swelling causing compartment syndrome

PMC4100269.diagnosis: Lipoprotein X-mediated extreme hypercholesterolemia from cholestatic obstructive jaundice due to a porta hepatis mass causing common bile duct stricture
rationale_summary: Marked cholestatic liver profile with progressive jaundice and pruritus
Imaging (US/CT/MRCP/ERCP) shows abrupt mid-CBD occlusion/tight 6 cm proximal CBD stricture with severe biliary dilatation from a porta hepatis mass
Total cholesterol and LDL >1,000 mg/dL with only mildly elevated triglycerides, occurring in the setting of cholestasis
After ERCP stenting and improvement of jaundice, serum cholesterol levels steadily declined, consistent with cholestasis-associated (Lp-X) hypercholesterolemia rather than primary dyslipidemia

PMC9723480.diagnosis: Sporotrichoid cutaneous nontuberculous mycobacterial infection (most consistent with Mycobacterium marinum)
rationale_summary: Painful, tender erythematous nodules in a linear (lymphangitic/sporotrichoid) distribution from hand to arm with hand edema
Skin biopsy showed granulomatous inflammation with acid-fast bacilli
Slit-skin smear for Mycobacterium leprae and sputum AFB were negative, arguing against leprosy or pulmonary TB as the source

Clinical deterioration stopped prompting discontinuation of flecainide in the setting of a tight temporal association

PMC10310912.diagnosis: Occult contained duodenal injury (microperforation) during cholecystectomy
rationale_summary: Gallbladder was densely adherent to the duodenum and required separation; the duodenum showed patchy bile discoloration.
IOC showed an unexpected streak of contrast just inferior to the cystic duct, suggesting a transient contrast leak near the duodenum.
No leak was seen with pressurized methylene blue and the contrast streak disappeared on repeat IOC 10 minutes later, consistent with a small contained/temporarily sealed duodenal injury.

Persistent fever and worsening systemic illness despite broad-spectrum antibiotics with negative blood cultures and normal procalcitonin

rationale_summary: Symptoms began shortly after dose escalation of an antipsychotic (perospirone 40 mg/day)
Hyperthermia (39.8 °C), severe extrapyramidal symptoms (tremor, rigidity, akathisia), altered mental status (restlessness, confusion)
Autonomic instability (tachycardia, fluctuating blood pressure, heavy sweating)
Laboratory evidence of muscle injury and systemic stress: elevated CPK (813 U/L), elevated myoglobin (486 µg/L), leukocytosis (18.3 × 10^9/L)
CSF and neuroimaging normal; EEG diffuse slowing consistent with metabolic/toxic encephalopathy supporting NMS over CNS infection/structural lesion

PMC10849070.diagnosis: Arrhythmogenic left ventricular cardiomyopathy (left-dominant arrhythmogenic cardiomyopathy) with LV apical aneurysm
rationale_summary: Recurrent sustained ventricular tachycardia with LBBB morphology and inferoapical origin on ECG
Imaging shows progressive LV apical thinning/bulging evolving to a true LV apical aneurysm with akinesis/paradoxical systolic motion; right ventricle remains normal
CMRI demonstrates fatty infiltration at the LV apex with late gadolinium enhancement, consistent with fibro-fatty myocardial replacement
Normal coronary angiography and repeatedly normal cardiac enzymes argue against ischemic infarction as the cause of the aneurysm/VT

PMC8299630.diagnosis: Secondary membranous nephropathy (HIV-associated immune complex kidney disease)
rationale_summary: Nephrotic-range proteinuria (24-h urine protein 4.35 g) with microscopic hematuria and preserved kidney function (creatinine 98 μmol/L)
Serum anti-PLA2R negative, supporting secondary rather than primary membranous nephropathy
History of chronic viral infection (transfusion-acquired HIV) known to be associated with secondary membranous/immune-complex glomerulopathy
Alternative secondary causes in the case are less supported: cryoglobulins negative and hepatitis C previously treated; SPEP without monoclonal bands; ANCA/dsDNA/anti-GBM negative

PMC9523897.diagnosis: Primary cutaneous aspergillosis of the lower lip
rationale_summary: 10-month slowly enlarging, painful, thick-crusted ulcer on the lower lip with serous discharge and progressive worsening despite topical antifungal/antimicrobial care, antibiotics, and empiric immunosuppression
Repeated biopsies showed marked reactive epidermal changes with mixed dermal inflammation but no malignant transformation; direct immunofluorescence negative (argues against immunobullous disease)

Leishmaniasis smear negative and no systemic immunodeficiency identified (HIV negative; nitroblue tetrazolium test normal), supporting an atypical localized opportunistic fungal infection rather than neoplasm, leishmaniasis, or autoimmune blistering disease

MRI notes a subtle cystic area in the region of the aqueduct, consistent with an intraventricular cyst obstructing CSF flow

CSF has markedly elevated opening pressure with lymphocyte/monocyte-predominant pleocytosis and mildly low glucose; routine Gram stain/India ink and bacterial/viral/fungal/mycobacterial cultures are negative
Born in Mexico (endemic area for Taenia solium), supporting neurocysticercosis as a cause of an intraventricular cyst and hydrocephalus

PMC11126872.diagnosis: Pediatric scurvy (vitamin C deficiency) with subperiosteal hemorrhage/pseudotumor of the femur
rationale_summary: Minimal fruit/vegetable intake and malnourished appearance (weight <1st percentile)
Hemorrhagic gingival and labial findings consistent with impaired collagen/bleeding tendency
Severe limb pain worse at night/inactivity with difficulty walking and joints held flexed, compatible with painful subperiosteal bleeding
Thigh swelling with a firm tender "mass" without erythema, suggesting hematoma/pseudotumor rather than infection
Normal coagulation studies and metabolic panel despite bleeding signs, supporting a nutritional deficiency etiology

PMC10308979.diagnosis: Complex regional pain syndrome (CRPS) type I after knee surgery
rationale_summary: Developed persistent/recurrent severe anterior knee pain (up to 10/10) with marked stiffness/limited ROM weeks after surgery
Autonomic/skin changes with episodic warmth, swelling/effusion, and color change/rash (erythematous then reticular/mottled) involving the limb

Repeated evaluations showed no infection (afebrile, normal inflammatory markers, MRI negative) and no vascular cause (patent vasculature), supporting a noninfectious postoperative pain dysregulation syndrome

PMC9361806.diagnosis: Transfusion-related acute lung injury (TRALI) causing acute noncardiogenic pulmonary edema/ARDS
rationale_summary: Acute, severe tachypnea progressing to hypoxemia requiring intubation shortly after receiving two pRBC transfusions
Large volume of serosanguineous/icteric fluid recovered via endotracheal tube consistent with acute pulmonary edema/lung injury

Pretransfusion ultrasound showed volume contraction and no pulmonary B-lines/effusion, arguing against preexisting cardiogenic pulmonary edema and supporting an acute transfusion-associated lung injury

PMC10073084.diagnosis: Primary lung adenocarcinoma with ovarian metastasis (ovarian metastatic tumor presenting as a large solid-cystic pelvic mass)
rationale_summary: CT showed a solid irregular lesion in the left upper lung lobe with ipsilateral pleural effusion and multiple small bilateral lung nodules, consistent with advanced primary lung malignancy
Concurrent very large solid-cystic pelvic/ovarian mass with bilateral ovarian cysts suggests metastatic spread to the ovaries rather than an isolated benign gynecologic process

PMC6656673.diagnosis: Primary familial brain calcification (Fahr disease) presenting with dementia
rationale_summary: Progressive cognitive decline over 5 years with functional impairment and low MMSE (19/30)
Noncontrast CT shows symmetric calcifications in basal ganglia (globus pallidus), thalami (pulvinar), and cortical/subcortical regions
Normal parathyroid hormone, calcium, phosphate, and thyroid studies argue against secondary (metabolic/endocrine) causes of intracranial calcification
Family history of documented brain calcifications in a brother supports a familial primary brain calcification disorder

PMC8557040.diagnosis: Renal replacement lipomatosis (advanced renal sinus lipomatosis) of the right kidney due to chronic obstruction/infection

PMC5745655.diagnosis: Protracted benzodiazepine withdrawal syndrome (after clonazepam discontinuation)
rationale_summary: Long-term clonazepam use (up to 1 mg twice daily) followed by taper/discontinuation
Recurrence of prominent autonomic and anxiety symptoms after stopping benzodiazepines (diaphoresis, palpitations, tremulousness, insomnia, restlessness, irritability, muscle tension)
Severity led to suicidal ideation and repeated psychiatric hospitalizations despite multiple failed/intolerated non-benzodiazepine treatments
Symptoms improved after gabapentin was initiated specifically for benzodiazepine withdrawal and titrated to 600 mg three times daily

PMC12016228.diagnosis: Central nervous system involvement of relapsing polychondritis (autoimmune recurrent aseptic lymphocytic meningitis/meningoencephalitis with leptomeningitis)
rationale_summary: Known relapsing polychondritis (auricular/nasal/laryngotracheal chondritis; auricular deformity on exam)
Multiple prior episodes of aseptic lymphocytic meningitis with progressive cognitive decline
Current episode with headache, fever, altered mental status and lymphocytic CSF pleocytosis with extensive negative infectious PCR/cultures (including ruling out Mollaret meningitis)
MRI shows diffuse nodular leptomeningeal enhancement with parenchymal nodular foci and hydrocephalus, supporting inflammatory leptomeningitis
Malignancy and alternative systemic inflammatory causes (sarcoidosis, IgG4-related disease, vasculitis, Sjögren's, SLE/RA) were investigated and not supported

PMC5477273.diagnosis: Congenital hypofibrinogenemia (fibrinogen deficiency) causing hemorrhage (retrobulbar hematoma)
rationale_summary: Recurrent excessive bleeding with very prolonged PT/aPTT during prior minor trauma/surgery
Current extensive hemostatic profile shows severe hypofibrinogenemia (fibrinogen 0.34 g/L) confirmed by repeated testing and near-absent Millar heat-precipitation band
Normal platelet count and buccal mucosal bleeding time argue against primary platelet/von Willebrand disorders
Normal D-dimers and antithrombin with only borderline FDPs make active DIC unlikely, supporting a primary fibrinogen deficiency

PMC8876503.diagnosis: Prednisolone-induced drug-induced liver injury (acute severe hepatitis)

PMC5743514.diagnosis: Wernicke encephalopathy due to thiamine deficiency from prolonged total parenteral nutrition after liver transplantation

PMC6510746.diagnosis: Primary giant cell tumor of the breast (extraosseous/soft-tissue type with osteoclast-like giant cells)
rationale_summary: Breast biopsies twice showed spindle/oval mononuclear cells admixed with osteoclast-like multinucleated giant cells, hyalinized stroma, lymphocytic infiltration, and hemorrhage without necrosis
Giant cells were strongly CD68-positive and mononuclear cells weakly CD68-positive, supporting a histiocytic/giant-cell tumor rather than epithelial carcinoma
No atypical epithelial cells were identified in the lesion (only rare non-epithelial atypical cells in a duct), arguing against metaplastic carcinoma
Axillary node FNA yielded only normal lymphocytes despite radiologic suspicion of nodal metastasis

PMC10876917.diagnosis: Autosomal recessive hypercholesterolemia (LDLRAP1-related severe familial hypercholesterolemia)
rationale_summary: Recurrent tendon and nodular xanthomas beginning before age 10, with markedly thickened Achilles tendons (15 mm).
Severe isolated LDL hypercholesterolemia (LDL-C 357 mg/dL; triglycerides 136 mg/dL), consistent with familial hypercholesterolemia phenotype.

Alternative xanthoma disorders are less supported: no palmar xanthomas and triglycerides not elevated (argues against dysbetalipoproteinemia); sitosterol is not elevated (argues against sitosterolemia); lysosomal acid lipase activity is normal (argues against LAL deficiency).

Despite childhood xanthomas and very high LDL-C, coronary CTA at age 29 shows no coronary artery disease, a pattern compatible with ARH's often less aggressive early atherosclerosis compared with classic homozygous LDLR-negative FH.

PMC8318494.diagnosis: Isolated unilateral absence (agenesis) of the right pulmonary artery causing severe pulmonary arterial hypertension
rationale_summary: Progressive dyspnea with JVD, edema, loud P2, RV enlargement/dysfunction, and catheterization showing severe precapillary pulmonary hypertension (mPAP 74 mm Hg, PAWP 12 mm Hg, PVR 35.4 WU, low cardiac index).
Perfusion scan shows absent right-lung perfusion with normal left-lung perfusion.

Pulmonary angiography and 4D CT demonstrate complete absence of the right pulmonary artery from the main pulmonary trunk and systemic collateral supply (lateral thoracic and inferior phrenic arteries) to an underdeveloped right lung, with no features of chronic thromboembolic disease.

PMC7857373.diagnosis: Classical Hodgkin lymphoma presenting with cervical/mediastinal lymphadenopathy causing superior vena cava syndrome and extensive central venous thrombosis
rationale_summary: Firm right cervical lymphadenopathy with additional pretracheal and mediastinal lymph nodes on CT
Systemic inflammatory features (1 month fever, ESR 120 mm/hr, CRP positive)
Suggestive lymphoma-associated labs (elevated IgE 779 IU/mL with eosinophils present)

CT venogram shows compression of right internal jugular vein by enlarged node plus thrombus extending through right internal jugular/subclavian/brachiocephalic veins into the SVC with extensive collateral veins (SVC syndrome)
Pulmonary/serosal involvement compatible with mediastinal lymphoproliferative disease (right middle lobe collapse/consolidation, small pleural effusion, mild pericardial effusion)
Infectious evaluations including TB GeneXpert/cultures and sputum studies were negative, and FNAC was nondiagnostic (can miss lymphoma)

PMC6259034.diagnosis: Bilateral renal artery stenosis/occlusion from fibromuscular dysplasia causing ischemic nephropathy with recurrent flash pulmonary edema (Pickering syndrome)
rationale_summary: 25-year-old woman with abrupt severe, drug-resistant hypertension and no atherosclerotic risk factors or endocrine features

Imaging shows severe left renal artery near-occlusion and right renal artery stenosis with otherwise normal aorta/branches, plus small hypoperfused left kidney-pattern consistent with renovascular disease from FMD
Progression to malignant hypertension with recurrent flash pulmonary edema and marked creatinine rises despite preserved urine output supports bilateral renovascular ischemia
Right-kidney biopsy shows viable/normal tissue and CTA shows extensive collaterals, consistent with potentially reversible ischemic nephropathy rather than intrinsic renal parenchymal disease

PMC4082171.diagnosis: Synthetic cannabinoid ("Spice/K2") intoxication causing multisystem toxicity (seizure, acute kidney injury, and transient cardiomyopathy with pulmonary edema/ARDS)
rationale_summary: Young man with witnessed tonic-clonic seizure followed by severe agitation/confusion and gastrointestinal symptoms
Routine tox screen negative for cocaine/PCP/opioids/amphetamines, but positive for cannabinoids (consistent with cannabinoid exposure while many synthetics are not detected)
Developed rapidly progressive acute kidney injury (creatinine 1.4→4.3 mg/dL and rising) with subsequent hypoxic respiratory failure and bilateral alveolar infiltrates
Echocardiography showed new global LV systolic dysfunction (EF 30%), supporting toxin-related cardiomyopathy and fluid overload contributing to respiratory failure
Marked clinical improvement in oxygenation, urine output, and creatinine after hemodialysis, consistent with a reversible toxic exposure with secondary volume overload

PMC10688243.diagnosis: Primary endometrial adenocarcinoma with intestinal (enteric) differentiation (high-grade mucinous/intestinal-type), mimicking metastatic colorectal carcinoma
rationale_summary: Polypoid mass arises in endometrium on hysteroscopy with residual superficial endometrial lesion and no myometrial/adnexal involvement on ultrasound
Tumor shows mucinous adenocarcinoma morphology with high-grade features (solid areas, necrosis, high mitotic rate)
Immunoprofile is enteric (diffuse CK20 and CDX2) but with Müllerian support (focal PAX8 and CK7) and ER/PR negativity
Extensive evaluation (EGD, colonoscopy, PET, MRI) found no gastrointestinal or other primary site, arguing against metastasis
p53 wild-type and intact MMR proteins are compatible with a primary endometrial carcinoma subtype rather than mismatch-repair-deficient metastatic colorectal cancer

PMC10888719.diagnosis: Neuromyelitis optica spectrum disorder (AQP4-IgG-associated) presenting with longitudinally extensive transverse myelitis and optic neuritis after COVID-19 vaccination
rationale_summary: Acute bilateral eye pain with MRI lesions in optic nerve/optic chiasm and delayed visual evoked potentials (optic neuritis)
Severe myelopathy with dysuria and rapid paraplegia; spinal MRI shows longitudinally extensive intramedullary T2 hyperintense lesion from T2-T12
CSF largely unremarkable aside from elevated protein, compatible with NMOSD presentation
Onset 10 days after Pfizer-BioNTech booster with negative infectious PCR tests supports a post-vaccination autoimmune demyelinating syndrome

PMC6088471.diagnosis: IgG4-related disease presenting as chronic otitis media with effusion (IgG4-related otitis media/Eustachian tube-nasopharyngeal involvement)
rationale_summary: Persistent unilateral otitis media with thick viscid red-yellow effusion and ongoing otorrhea despite ventilation tubes, with negative swab and nonspecific nasopharyngeal biopsy

MRI showed extensive mucosal inflammation/enhancement from middle ear and mastoid extending along the Eustachian tube into the nasopharynx and sinonasal cavity, without a clear infectious or atopic cause

PMC6099017.diagnosis: Primary hepatic T-cell-rich B-cell lymphoma (B-cell lymphoma with marked reactive T-cell infiltrate)
rationale_summary: Hepatic mass aspirate cytology interpreted as lymphoma with mixed lymphoid population and lymphoglandular background

PMC10300258.diagnosis: Myelin oligodendrocyte glycoprotein antibody-associated disease (MOGAD) with ADEM-like encephalitis and bilateral optic neuritis/chiasmitis
rationale_summary: Subacute headaches and seizure with markedly raised CSF opening pressure but otherwise near-normal CSF and negative HSV PCR, making herpes encephalitis unlikely

MRI shows multifocal bilateral T2/FLAIR hyperintense, mildly expansile lesions (temporal/insular with extension to corpus callosum, deep gray, and periventricular white matter) without diffusion restriction, enhancement, or hemorrhage-pattern consistent with inflammatory demyelination

Severe bilateral progressive visual loss with optic disc swelling on OCT and absent visual evoked potentials, plus optic chiasm involvement on MRI, supporting bilateral optic neuritis/chiasmitis typical of MOGAD

PMC11388267.diagnosis: Intermittent small-bowel (ileal) intussusception due to a terminal ileum intramural lipoma
rationale_summary: Acute severe cramping periumbilical/right iliac pain with nausea/vomiting and prior 3-month history of intermittent right-sided abdominal discomfort suggests intermittent mechanical obstruction
Abdominal radiograph showed small-bowel distension with absence of ileocecal gas, supporting distal small-bowel obstruction
CT angiography identified an intramural lipoma in the terminal ileum, a typical lead point for adult intussusception
Colonoscopy could not be advanced beyond the terminal ileum, consistent with a distal ileal obstructing process despite otherwise normal endoscopy

PMC3130996.diagnosis: HSV-associated sacral radiculitis causing postherpetic neuralgia (neuropathic pain following genital herpes)
rationale_summary: Acute bilateral vulval blisters diagnosed as genital herpes responded rapidly to valacyclovir
Persistent burning pain and marked hyperaesthesia in a dermatomal S1-S3 distribution without motor deficit, consistent with neuropathic/radicular pain after herpes infection
No recurrent ulcers during the initial prolonged pain episode and minimal response to extended antiviral therapy, arguing against ongoing mucocutaneous infection
Recurrence of vulval blisters was followed again by identical dermatomal hyperaesthesia, supporting an HSV-triggered neuralgia
Pain resolved with low-dose amitriptyline on both episodes, consistent with postherpetic-type neuropathic pain

PMC11146635.diagnosis: Sellar-suprasellar germinoma (intracranial germ cell tumor) causing hypopituitarism and central diabetes insipidus

PMC10502199.diagnosis: Granulocyte colony-stimulating factor (G-CSF)-associated large-vessel vasculitis (aortitis with carotid arteritis)
rationale_summary: Neck pain and swelling localized to the left carotid bifurcation with fever and markedly elevated CRP (328 mg/L)
Imaging showed circumferential inflammatory thickening around the left internal/common carotid bifurcation and concentric thickening of the aortic arch (large-vessel involvement)
Symptoms occurred shortly after receiving G-CSF (4 days prior) in the setting of chemotherapy-associated neutropenia

Pericarotid cytopunctures showed inflammatory cells (neutrophils/lymphocytes) without malignant cells or giant cells, supporting an inflammatory vasculitic process rather than tumor or classic giant-cell-rich arteritis on sampling

PMC8414607.diagnosis: Delayed post-hypoxic leukoencephalopathy (delayed toxic-hypoxic leukoencephalopathy after overdose/respiratory failure)
rationale_summary: Had a hypoxic/toxic precipitant (suspected opioid/benzodiazepine overdose with aspiration pneumonia and acute respiratory failure requiring intubation/ICU stay)
After apparent medical recovery, developed a delayed neuropsychiatric deterioration within weeks (behavioral disinhibition progressing to near akinetic mutism)

Brain MRI repeatedly showed extensive, diffuse, nonenhancing cerebral white-matter T2 hyperintensities (with basal ganglia involvement) without restricted diffusion, fitting a demyelinating leukoencephalopathy pattern
CSF studies were broadly negative except mild protein elevation, and EEG showed diffuse slowing-both consistent with toxic/hypoxic encephalopathy rather than infectious/autoimmune causes
No improvement with high-dose steroids or IVIG, arguing against steroid-responsive autoimmune encephalopathy (e.g., SREAT)

PMC6010628.diagnosis: Primary lung squamous cell carcinoma presenting as pneumonic-type (lepidic/aerogenous spread) consolidation
rationale_summary: Elderly heavy smoker with markedly elevated squamous markers (squamous cell carcinoma antigen, cytokeratin 19 fragment) and elevated CEA
CT shows a large right lower-lobe consolidation with air bronchograms (pneumonic-type appearance) and FDG-PET demonstrates high uptake (SUVmax up to 13.03) consistent with malignancy

Transbronchial biopsy/bronchial brushing cytology was interpreted as squamous cell carcinoma; admixture of occasional ciliated and mucin-containing columnar cells can occur from entrapped/reactive airway epithelium in a consolidative lesion
Background bilateral reticular opacities with honeycombing suggests coexisting interstitial lung disease, a known setting for lung cancer

PMC10810688.diagnosis: X-linked hypophosphatemia (FGF23-mediated renal phosphate wasting causing osteomalacia/enthesopathy, mimicking ankylosing spondylitis)
rationale_summary: Persistent symptoms despite NSAIDs and adalimumab with HLA-B27 negativity make inflammatory ankylosing spondylitis atypical
Chronic hypophosphatemia (phosphate 1.9 mg/dL) with elevated alkaline phosphatase and elevated PTH suggests osteomalacia physiology rather than primary spondyloarthritis
24-hour urine phosphate 1300 mg is inappropriately normal for hypophosphatemia, supporting renal phosphate wasting (FGF23-mediated disorder)

Clinical features characteristic of XLH: diffuse enthesophytes/ligament calcification, femoral bowing with subtrochanteric insufficiency fractures, long-standing dental abscesses/fractures/extractions, and progressive hearing loss since childhood

PMC4010020.diagnosis: Late-onset urea cycle disorder (most consistent with ornithine transcarbamylase deficiency) causing noncirrhotic hyperammonemic encephalopathy
rationale_summary: Severe hyperammonemia (202→516 µmol/L) with progressive encephalopathy/coma and triphasic waves on EEG

PMC7869131.diagnosis: Williams-Campbell syndrome (congenital deficiency of bronchial cartilage causing cystic bronchiectasis with expiratory bronchomalacia)
rationale_summary: Recurrent pneumonias since childhood with bronchiectasis and infections including Nocardia, Moraxella, and MAC
CT shows multiple ballooning cystic bronchiectasis distal to the third-generation bronchi, involving 4th-6th order bronchi
Marked expiratory collapse of the bronchiectatic airways on end-expiratory CT, consistent with bronchomalacia from absent/weak cartilage
Hyperinflation and progressive fixed obstructive physiology without bronchodilator reversibility
Alternative causes of bronchiectasis were not supported: normal immunoglobulins, normal α-1 antitrypsin, normal sweat chloride, negative autoimmune and Aspergillus testing

PMC10943559.diagnosis: Adenosine-sensitive para-Hisian (septal right atrial) microreentrant atrial tachycardia
rationale_summary: Regular narrow-QRS long-RP SVT with earliest atrial activation at the His-bundle region
Adenosine terminated the tachycardia without atrioventricular block, supporting an atrial tachycardia mechanism rather than AV-node-dependent reentry

PMC11625115.diagnosis: Primary spinal chondrosarcoma (thoracic, involving T5-T6 with rib/costovertebral extension)
rationale_summary: 36-year-old with 3-year progressive back mass and pain with developing thoracic myelopathy (bilateral weakness, sensory level, Babinski).
Imaging shows a large expansile lytic-sclerotic lesion centered in the right posterolateral T5-T6 vertebrae with extension into transverse processes, costovertebral junction, and ribs 5-6.
CT demonstrates a chondroid matrix with calcification, supporting a cartilage-forming malignant tumor.

MRI shows a heterogeneous mass with necrosis and aggressive local behavior (spinal canal stenosis, posterior ligament obliteration, intradural/intramedullary invasion, thoracic cavity extension), favoring chondrosarcoma over benign osteochondroma.

PMC10460928.diagnosis: Adult-onset Still's disease complicated by macrophage activation syndrome (secondary hemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis)
rationale_summary: Daily high-grade fevers with inflammatory arthritis (swollen/tender wrists and PIP joints) and diffuse pruritic maculopapular rash with linear erythematous plaques
Marked hyperferritinemia (>15,000 ng/mL) with very high IL-18 and soluble IL-2 receptor, supporting Still's disease with hyperinflammatory syndrome
Cytopenias (Hgb 8.6, platelets 115), severe liver injury/cholestasis, hypofibrinogenemia (81 mg/dL) and extreme D-dimer elevation consistent with macrophage activation syndrome/secondary HLH

Negative ANA/RF/ANCA and broad negative infectious workup (cultures, viral testing, TEE without vegetations) argue against autoimmune connective tissue disease or active infection as the primary cause

PMC4035763.diagnosis: Metformin-associated lactic acidosis (MALA) precipitated by acute kidney injury/dehydration
rationale_summary: On metformin 3 g daily with 15-year type 2 diabetes
Profound high-anion gap metabolic acidosis (pH 6.58, HCO3 ~1-3.6 mmol/L, anion gap 55) with very high lactate (16.7 mmol/L)
Severe acute renal failure (creatinine 768 µmol/L, urea 30.8 mmol/L) consistent with reduced metformin clearance
Hypotension and poor oral intake/diarrhea for 3 weeks consistent with dehydration precipitating AKI
Exploratory laparotomy found no ischemic bowel despite presumed diagnosis, supporting a non-surgical cause of lactatemia

PMC6141727.diagnosis: Pityriasis rubra pilaris (PRP) with mucosal and esophageal involvement
rationale_summary: Follicular hyperkeratotic plaques with open comedones and papulopustules on trunk/limbs are characteristic of PRP
Well-demarcated erythematous plaques on palms and soles fit PRP-associated palmoplantar involvement
Prominent mucositis (diffuse oral erythema, glazed tongue, cheilitis) with vulvar erythema and esophageal linear ulcerations can occur as PRP mucosal involvement
Negative direct/indirect immunofluorescence and absent pemphigus/paraneoplastic pemphigus antibodies argue against autoimmune blistering disease
Normal zinc/vitamin studies and normal glucagon with normal pancreatic imaging argue against nutritional deficiency or glucagonoma-related necrolytic migratory erythema

PMC5995707.diagnosis: Takayasu arteritis with coronary artery involvement causing critical left main trunk stenosis (acute coronary syndrome/cardiogenic shock)

PMC7303445.diagnosis: Thromboangiitis obliterans (Buerger disease) with temporal artery involvement

Hand duplex/angiography shows segmental occlusions of ulnar/palmar/digital arteries with a corkscrew pattern-classic for thromboangiitis obliterans
Inflammatory markers (ESR/CRP) normal and autoimmune/vasculitis serologies (ANA, RF, ANCA, complements) unremarkable, supporting a non-atherosclerotic, non-immune systemic vasculitis process
Superficial temporal artery enlargement/stenosis on ultrasound can occur as an unusual site of involvement in Buerger disease, mimicking temporal arteritis

PMC11802228.diagnosis: Miller Fisher syndrome (anti-GQ1b-spectrum postinfectious neuropathy) triggered by herpes zoster ophthalmicus
rationale_summary: Abrupt onset of painful diplopia with multiple ocular motor palsies (right abduction most affected with mild adduction/elevation weakness) after recent VZV infection/meningitis
Generalized hyporeflexia with no structural lesion on contrast MRI (no orbital apex mass, no cranial nerve enhancement, no brainstem abnormalities)

Repeat CSF at onset of ophthalmoplegia showed normal cell count with elevated protein (albuminocytologic dissociation) and VZV PCR negative, supporting immune-mediated neuropathy rather than active VZV
Blink reflex abnormality (absent right R1 and prolonged bilateral R2) consistent with cranial nerve/brainstem reflex pathway involvement seen in GBS/Miller Fisher spectrum

PMC4528349.diagnosis: Ectopic ACTH syndrome from an ACTH-secreting intra-abdominal neuroendocrine tumor (small-bowel/mesenteric carcinoid)
rationale_summary: Overt Cushingoid features with elevated urinary free cortisol and loss of diurnal cortisol rhythm
Failure to suppress cortisol with 1 mg overnight dexamethasone and plasma ACTH not suppressed (32.6 pg/mL) indicates ACTH-dependent hypercortisolism
Repeated pituitary MRI reported normal and prior inferior petrosal sinus catheterization failed to localize a pituitary ACTH source
Large enhancing intra-abdominal mass adjacent to small bowel provides a plausible ectopic ACTH source

PMC6258140.diagnosis: Bilateral proliferative ischemic retinopathy from calcific uremic arteriolopathy (ocular calciphylaxis) in end-stage renal disease

Marked bilateral retinal/choroidal ischemia: rubeosis iridis, optic-disc neovascularization with leakage, and extensive peripheral capillary nonperfusion on fluorescein angiography
OCT shows hyperreflective arterial vessel walls and EDI-OCT shows thickened choroidal vessel walls with lumenal enlargement, supporting vascular wall pathology such as calcification

Doppler shows reduced ophthalmic/central retinal artery flow despite only mild carotid stenosis, favoring a local occlusive vasculopathy rather than severe carotid disease

PMC7883144.diagnosis: Nitrofurantoin-induced acute hypersensitivity reaction (sepsis-like systemic inflammatory response with pleuritis/hypotension)
rationale_summary: Abrupt onset of fever, chills, pleuritic chest pain/congestion, leukocytosis, and hypotension within <24 hours of first nitrofurantoin exposure

PMC8426173.diagnosis: Scabies (Sarcoptes scabiei infestation with scabetic hypersensitivity)
rationale_summary: Intensely pruritic, progressively generalized eczematous plaques

PMC5956711.diagnosis: X-linked incomplete congenital stationary night blindness (CACNA1F-related retinopathy)
rationale_summary: Early-onset nystagmus and abnormal visual behavior with family history of nyctalopia/congenital stationary night blindness in paternal relatives

Full-field ERG shows rod dysfunction greater than cone dysfunction with an electronegative mixed rod-cone response (reduced b-to-a ratio), consistent with a retinal synaptic/ON-bipolar transmission disorder
OCT demonstrates foveal hypoplasia without structural progression over years, supporting a congenital/stationary retinopathy rather than a primary degenerative dystrophy
TRPM1 testing was negative, making autosomal-recessive complete CSNB less likely and favoring X-linked incomplete CSNB (e.g., CACNA1F) with cone involvement that can decline over time

PMC11469885.diagnosis: Secondary syphilis with syphilitic hepatitis and syphilis-associated nephrotic syndrome (membranous nephropathy)

PMC8564683.diagnosis: Caseous calcification of the mitral annulus (caseous mitral annular calcification)
rationale_summary: Echo-lucent, partially calcified 28×24×21 mm mass located at the posterolateral/posterior mitral annulus with no mitral stenosis or regurgitation
CMR shows a heterogeneous peri-annular mass with no first-pass perfusion and only peripheral late gadolinium enhancement, consistent with an avascular lesion with a calcified/fibrous rim
Native T1 and T2 mapping demonstrate an abnormal hyperintense, elevated central region compatible with caseous/liquefied core rather than solid tumor
No fever or laboratory/ECG evidence of acute coronary syndrome; transesophageal echocardiography excludes mobile vegetations, arguing against infective endocarditis/abscess

PMC10092393.diagnosis: Giant cystic schwannoma of the sciatic nerve (ancient/degenerative schwannoma)
rationale_summary: Mass present and slowly enlarging for 12 years (benign peripheral nerve sheath tumor behavior) with recent acute pain/expansion consistent with hemorrhagic degeneration
Posterior thigh location with proximal and distal sciatic nerve seen on imaging but the nerve not visualized at the level of the mass, suggesting the lesion arises from/engulfs the nerve

CT/MRI showed a very large multiloculated cystic lesion with fluid-fluid levels and a thick fibrous rim, compatible with a schwannoma with cystic/hemorrhagic degeneration rather than a simple hematoma

Repeated rapid reaccumulation of bloody fluid after aspiration and debridements with a rind-like capsule and peripheral solid hypertrophic tissue supports an underlying encapsulated tumor with hemorrhagic/cystic change

PMC11273566.diagnosis: Biliary tract foreign body (suspected grass awn) causing septic cholangitis/cholecystitis and extrahepatic biliary obstruction
rationale_summary: Ultrasound showed hyperechoic spindle-shaped structures with linear interfaces and shadowing within the common bile duct and extending to the duodenal papilla, suggestive of vegetal foreign bodies
Bile aspirate demonstrated septic suppurative inflammation and culture grew Escherichia coli
Biochemistry showed marked hepatocellular injury and cholestasis (severely increased ALT and hyperbilirubinaemia) with high serum amyloid A
Follow-up ultrasound suggested retrograde migration of the intrabiliary spindle-shaped structures despite medical therapy

PMC11783208.diagnosis: Torsion of a pedunculated subserosal uterine leiomyoma with ischemic infarction (with secondary right adnexal involvement and ureterohydronephrosis)
rationale_summary: Known uterine leiomyomas with acute right-sided lower abdominal pain and focal tenderness
MRI shows a solid mass arising from the right uterine cornua via a broad pedicle with the bridging-vessel sign, supporting pedunculated subserosal fibroid origin
Thickened pedicle twisted in a spiral configuration consistent with torsion
Post-contrast MRI shows largely nonenhancing mass with only a thin rim and small enhancing area near attachment, consistent with ischemia/infarction
Enlarged right ovary with heterogeneous signal, peripheral follicles, and stromal hemorrhagic signal suggests associated adnexal congestion/torsion
Twisted pedicle kinks the right ureter causing ipsilateral ureterohydronephrosis

PMC5918180.diagnosis: Antidromic atrioventricular reentrant tachycardia via a right-sided decremental (Mahaim/atriofascicular) accessory pathway
rationale_summary: Regular WCT with LBBB morphology and left superior axis, terminated by adenosine and ventricular pacing
No baseline preexcitation, but atrial pacing with abrupt switch to AV nodal slow pathway produced fusion then fully preexcited QRS with His potential displaced into the ventricular electrogram
Progressive prolongation of the stimulus-delta interval with incremental atrial pacing indicates a decremental antegrade accessory pathway (Mahaim physiology)
Retrograde conduction during induced arrhythmia was concentric and adenosine caused VA block, consistent with retrograde limb through the AV node rather than a retrograde accessory pathway
Mapping showed RV apex activation preceding tricuspid annulus only during preexcitation, supporting right-sided atriofascicular insertion

PMC10959544.diagnosis: Post-enucleation optic nerve stump cyst (cystic dilation of the residual optic nerve/optic nerve sheath)
rationale_summary: Remote right eye enucleation without orbital implant for retinoblastoma
New difficulty fitting prosthesis with a translucent, vascularized conjunctival bulge in an anophthalmic socket

CT/MRI show a nonenhancing bilobated ovoid cystic orbital structure whose T1 signal matches the atrophied optic nerve, supporting origin from the residual optic nerve stump rather than an expanding tumor
Lesion was not present on CT 6 years earlier, consistent with an acquired benign post-enucleation cyst

PMC8318999.diagnosis: Physiologic neonatal breast enlargement (neonatal mastauxe) with "witch's milk" secretion

Infant afebrile with normal vital signs and normal laboratory studies, arguing against infectious mastitis

PMC5368394.diagnosis: Mycobacterium marinum infection (fish-tank-associated cutaneous/tenosynovial disease with sporotrichoid nodules)
rationale_summary: Chronic progressive swelling/stiffness of hand and later elbow with firm nodules along extensor surfaces after finger laceration
Exposure risk: maintains multiple large hobby aquariums
Routine bacterial cultures repeatedly negative and poor response to standard antibiotics
Marked worsening of nodular lesions after escalation of immunosuppression including TNF-α inhibitors (adalimumab, etanercept), consistent with occult atypical mycobacterial infection

PMC3224435.diagnosis: Isolated pulmonary artery vasculitis (Takayasu-type large-vessel arteritis limited to the pulmonary arteries)
rationale_summary: Repeat CT and MRI pulmonary angiography showed bilateral proximal main pulmonary artery smooth, concentric soft-tissue/intimal thickening with focal stenosis/occlusion rather than embolic webs typical of CTEPH
Marked systemic inflammation (CRP 77 mg/L, ESR 79 mm/hr) with negative ANA/ANCA supports a non-ANCA large-vessel vasculitis process

FDG-PET showed only intermediate uptake (less supportive of pulmonary artery sarcoma) while hemodynamics showed pulmonary hypertension (mPAP 32 mmHg) consistent with flow-limiting inflammatory stenoses

PMC6519503.diagnosis: Left testicular seminoma with tumor thrombus extending along the spermatic cord and left gonadal vein, with metastatic disease to the left suprarenal (adrenal) region
rationale_summary: 70-year-old man with enlarging, painless left testicular mass and impalpable testis on exam
Tumor markers: AFP normal and β-hCG only minimally elevated with markedly elevated LDH-pattern compatible with seminoma
Ultrasound shows grossly enlarged heterogeneous testis with chaotic intratesticular vascularity consistent with malignancy
CT shows contiguous soft-tissue extension from testis through spermatic cord into the left gonadal vein up to the kidney level (tumor thrombus) plus a large left suprarenal mass
FDG-PET demonstrates intense uptake in the testicular lesion, venous extension, and suprarenal mass supporting metabolically active metastatic germ cell tumor

PMC11978730.diagnosis: Schwannoma of the celiac/lesser omental (perigastric) nerve plexus mimicking a gastric GIST
rationale_summary: Incidental, well-defined dumbbell-shaped soft-tissue mass near the stomach's lesser curvature with extraluminal location and no mucosal abnormality on endoscopy

3D reconstruction showed the lesion encircling major vessels (left gastric artery, splenic artery and vein), a pattern more typical of a neurogenic tumor arising from autonomic nerve plexus/lesser omentum than a primary gastric wall tumor
Normal tumor markers and lack of GI symptoms support a benign peripheral nerve sheath tumor presentation
