PMC7921180.diagnosis: Coral reef aorta (severe calcified visceral aortic atherosclerotic plaque) causing bilateral renal artery ostial occlusion
rationale_summary: Refractory hypertensive emergency with acute kidney injury requiring dialysis
CTA/aortography show a large segment of heavily calcified plaque in the visceral aorta (below SMA to IMA) involving both renal artery origins
Both renal arteries are occluded at their origins with distal reconstitution via collaterals, consistent with chronic ostial obstruction from calcific aortic plaque
Endovascular stenting deemed unfeasible due to extreme calcification, a feature of coral reef aorta

PMC8473659.diagnosis: Chronic nonbacterial osteomyelitis (chronic recurrent multifocal osteomyelitis, CRMO) with associated recurrent inflammatory optic neuropathy
rationale_summary: Recurrent, migratory bone pain with periosteal reactions in multiple sites and multifocal longitudinal linear uptake on bone scan (humeri, forearms, ribs, femora, tibiae).
Inflammatory labs (WBC 14,800; platelets 514,000; ESR 51; CRP 5) with negative TB test and unremarkable bone marrow biopsy, arguing against infection or malignancy.
Relapsing course over months/years with MRI showing periosteal reaction, soft-tissue rind, and bone marrow edema/enhancement consistent with sterile inflammatory osteitis.

Later recurrent bilateral optic neuritis/chiasmitis that was steroid-responsive and repeatedly AQP4-IgG and MOG-IgG negative, supporting an associated systemic inflammatory/autoinflammatory disorder rather than NMOSD/MOGAD.

PMC11109351.diagnosis: Cervicothoracic dumbbell schwannoma (spinal nerve sheath tumor)
rationale_summary: Progressive cervical myelopathy with exam localizing to cervical cord

No bony destruction or marrow abnormality, favoring a benign nerve sheath tumor over invasive malignancy

PMC9400789.diagnosis: Immune checkpoint inhibitor-related pseudoprogression (tumor flare) after pembrolizumab
rationale_summary: After first pembrolizumab dose the supraclavicular nodal mass rapidly enlarged with pain, erythema, and edema and ECOG worsened to 3, suggesting an acute inflammatory flare rather than steady tumor growth

CT before the second dose showed reduction of thoracic lymphadenopathy consistent with a RECIST partial response, supporting initial apparent progression as pseudoprogression

PMC4666013.diagnosis: Primary pigmented nodular adrenocortical disease (PPNAD) causing familial ACTH-independent Cushing syndrome (often within Carney complex)
rationale_summary: Both siblings have clinical Cushing syndrome with repeatedly elevated cortisol that does not suppress with low-dose dexamethasone and suppressed ACTH (<5 pg/mL), indicating ACTH-independent hypercortisolism
Brother's 6-day Liddle test shows a paradoxical increase in urinary free cortisol with dexamethasone, a characteristic finding of PPNAD
Adrenal imaging shows nodular/enlarged adrenal glands (brother) and a small adrenal nodule (sister), compatible with nodular adrenal disease rather than pituitary/ectopic ACTH
Young age with severe osteoporosis/fragility fracture supports an endogenous adrenal cause such as PPNAD

PMC3440930.diagnosis: Multilocular thymic cyst associated with HIV (acquired thymic cyst)
rationale_summary: Large lobulated anterior mediastinal mass composed of nonenhancing cystic areas with enhancing septations on contrast CT, consistent with a multiloculated cystic thymic lesion

Occurs in an HIV-infected patient and accompanied by lymphoid tissue enlargement (bilateral parotid swelling and cervical lymphadenopathy), supporting an HIV-associated lymphoid process with thymic cyst formation
No invasion of adjacent mediastinal structures, favoring a benign cystic thymic abnormality over invasive malignancy

PMC9208679.diagnosis: Primary pulmonary tuberculosis with necrotic mediastinal (paratracheal) lymphadenitis causing airway compression
rationale_summary: CT showed a rim-enhancing lobulated right paratracheal lesion contiguous with involved lung and extending into the posterior mediastinum, consistent with necrotic/rim-enhancing tuberculous lymphadenitis
Associated right upper lobe and superior right lower lobe consolidation with multiple cystic nonenhancing areas suggests parenchymal necrosis/caseation rather than uncomplicated bacterial pneumonia
Symptoms were subacute (2 weeks) with minimal systemic inflammatory response (no fever, low CRP) and radiographic severity disproportionate to clinical presentation
No visible BCG scar in an infant increases concern for tuberculosis susceptibility

PMC5120475.diagnosis: ROHHAD syndrome (Rapid-onset Obesity with Hypothalamic dysfunction, Hypoventilation, and Autonomic Dysregulation)

Prominent autonomic dysregulation: decreased lacrimation/nasal secretions, diminished sweating, cold blue extremities, alternating diarrhea/constipation, urinary incontinence
Hypothalamic-related features despite initially normal standard endocrine labs: polyuria/polydipsia, hypersomnolence with major behavioral/mood changes
Brain MRI showing pituitary gland enlargement without other structural brain abnormalities, supporting hypothalamic-pituitary involvement

PMC8065272.diagnosis: Renin-mediated secondary hypertension from intra-abdominal/renal compression due to abdominal packing (Page-kidney-like effect from perihepatic packs)
rationale_summary: Developed new severe, refractory and paroxysmal hypertensive crises after damage-control laparotomy with extensive abdominal packing (20 drapes) and +10 L fluid balance
Hypertension persisted despite multiple antihypertensives/diuretics and was not explained by pain after extubation

Workup excluded major alternative structural causes cited in the differential: CT angiography showed no aortic dissection, renal Doppler was normal, and CT showed no renal hematoma, supporting an external compression/compartment mechanism rather than vascular dissection or Page kidney from hematoma

rationale_summary: Decades-long course beginning with "map-like" hypopigmented patches on hands/wrists and progressive sensory symptoms
Marked peripheral neuropathy on exam (50% sensory loss to knees) with hand muscle atrophy and multiple contractures
Imaging shows severe distal phalangeal resorption (acro-osteolysis) and destructive lytic/erosive foot changes consistent with neuropathic bone/joint damage rather than inflammatory arthritis
Lack of typical systemic sclerosis features (no sclerodactyly, telangiectasias, perioral furrowing) despite acro-osteolysis supports an alternative cause such as leprosy

PMC7934766.diagnosis: Secondary (vascular) mania due to cerebral small-vessel ischemic disease
rationale_summary: New-onset manic/psychotic syndrome in late life (excessive/disorganized speech with flight of ideas, grandiose/paranoid delusions, impulsive spending, disinhibition, irritability) without a prior history of mania
Executive dysfunction out of proportion to global cognition (MMSE 27/30 with markedly low FAB 9/18; impaired inhibitory control, perseveration, impaired abstraction)

Neurologic and imaging evidence of cerebrovascular disease (broad-based shuffling gait; MRI with confluent periventricular/subcortical white-matter hyperintensities and advanced small-vessel ischemic disease with microbleeds, plus frontal-predominant atrophy)
Multiple vascular risk factors supporting a vascular etiology (hypertension, heavy smoking, LDL 164 mg/dL, 10-year ASCVD risk 34%)

PMC10832606.diagnosis: Desmoid-type fibromatosis (aggressive fibromatosis) of the groin causing common femoral vein compression
rationale_summary: Progressive unilateral non-pitting right leg edema with duplex excluding DVT suggests extrinsic venous/lymphatic obstruction
CT/duplex/MRI show a 3-5 cm solid lesion dorsal to the common femoral vein producing focal venous narrowing

MRI features (T1 isointense to muscle, mildly T2 hyperintense, slight contrast enhancement) and fine-needle biopsy showing proliferating spindle cells fit a fibrous spindle-cell lesion such as desmoid-type fibromatosis

PMC3978404.diagnosis: Constrictive pericarditis causing congestive hepatopathy (cardiac cirrhosis)
rationale_summary: Incidentally noted nodular liver with biopsy showing cirrhosis and sinusoidal dilatation, consistent with hepatic congestion
Liver serologies for viral, autoimmune, and metabolic causes were normal, making primary hepatic etiologies less likely

Echocardiography showed an enlarged inferior vena cava and right-heart catheterization showed elevated filling pressures (RA 12 mm Hg, wedge 18 mm Hg, LVEDP 18 mm Hg) supporting systemic venous congestion
CT pulmonary angiography ruled out pulmonary embolism and showed scattered pericardial calcifications, supporting constrictive pericardial disease
Progressive exertional dyspnea developed in parallel with signs of congestive physiology

PMC10233214.diagnosis: Multisystem inflammatory syndrome in adults (MIS-A) following prior SARS-CoV-2 infection
rationale_summary: Young adult with acute high fever followed by rapid deterioration to shock and hypoxemic respiratory failure requiring ICU vasopressors
Marked hyperinflammatory state (very high CRP, ferritin, IL-6, D-dimer) with thrombocytopenia, coagulopathy, and multiorgan injury (AKI, transaminitis)
Infectious workup negative (SARS-CoV-2 PCR negative; respiratory viral panel, cultures, and atypical pathogens negative)
Evidence of recent SARS-CoV-2 exposure/immunity (PCR-confirmed infection 8 weeks earlier; SARS-CoV-2 IgG positive) consistent with a post-infectious inflammatory syndrome

PMC6751912.diagnosis: Late-onset ornithine transcarbamylase (OTC) deficiency causing hyperammonemic encephalopathy (valproate-precipitated) mimicking non-convulsive status epilepticus
rationale_summary: 5-year history of recurrent episodes of prolonged akinetic mutism/coma with post-episode amnesia suggesting episodic metabolic encephalopathy
EEG showed generalized 2.5 Hz sharp-and-slow discharges with poor sustained clinical response to multiple antiseizure and anesthetic agents
Developed hyperammonemia with normal liver-function tests, pointing to a non-hepatic cause of hyperammonemia (e.g., urea cycle disorder)
Valproate (and topiramate) were given and ammonia rose, consistent with valproate precipitating hyperammonemic crisis in an underlying urea cycle defect

Treatment shifted to dextrose plus ammonia scavengers (sodium benzoate/phenylbutyrate) and hemodialysis; she developed cerebral edema and near-isoelectric EEG consistent with severe hyperammonemic encephalopathy

PMC9941870.diagnosis: Desmoplastic fibroblastoma (collagenous fibroma) of the breast
rationale_summary: Core biopsy showed a hypocellular proliferation of bland spindle cells in collagenous and focally myxoid stroma without breast glandular elements
Spindle cells were negative for cytokeratin 5/6 and p63, arguing against epithelial/metaplastic carcinoma
β-catenin lacked nuclear staining (only cytoplasmic), supporting a benign fibrous spindle-cell lesion rather than desmoid-type fibromatosis

PMC7403879.diagnosis: Calcified broad ligament leiomyoma (extrauterine uterine fibroid) mimicking an adnexal mass
rationale_summary: Pelvic ultrasound showed a lobulated solid, partially calcified mass with color flow in the posterior cul-de-sac that appeared adjacent to but separate from the right ovary

Prior pelvic ultrasounds 9 years earlier documented a similar heterogeneous solid right adnexal mass and specifically raised the possibility of a broad ligament fibroid, supporting a long-standing benign leiomyoma

PMC11116953.diagnosis: Idiopathic left posterior fascicular ventricular tachycardia occurring in a patient with Wolff-Parkinson-White (manifest right-sided accessory pathway)
rationale_summary: Wide-complex tachycardia with right bundle branch block morphology and superior axis in a structurally normal heart is characteristic of left posterior fascicular VT
Subtle beat-to-beat QRS duration/morphology variation supports ventricular origin rather than a fixed pre-excited supraventricular tachycardia
No response to adenosine (including no change in ventriculoatrial conduction) and failure of initial synchronized cardioversion argue against AV node-dependent SVT/antidromic AVRT

After cardioversion, sinus rhythm showed manifest ventricular pre-excitation with LBBB pattern that was distinctly different from the tachycardia QRS morphology, indicating a bystander accessory pathway rather than the tachycardia mechanism

PMC8904927.diagnosis: Drug-induced hypersensitivity myopericarditis (likely lenalidomide-associated) mimicking lateral STEMI
rationale_summary: Symptoms began 19 days after starting lenalidomide/bortezomib/dexamethasone, with pruritus from Day 8 and a widespread erythematous pruritic rash for 1 week (drug hypersensitivity timing)
Marked myocardial injury markers with high-sensitivity troponin T 5.47 µg/L and elevated NT-proBNP, with inflammatory response (CRP 47.4 mg/L)

ECG showed convex ST elevation in I/aVL with reciprocal inferior ST depression, but echocardiography showed normal ejection fraction and no regional wall motion abnormalities (argues against acute coronary occlusion, supports myocarditis)
Localized pericardial effusion (8 mm) on echocardiography supports pericardial involvement (myopericarditis)

PMC9522865.diagnosis: Tumor necrosis factor receptor-associated periodic syndrome (TRAPS)
rationale_summary: Recurrent stereotyped flares for 2 years with prodromal flu-like symptoms followed by fever, sore throat, arthralgias, severe headache, and intermittent diarrhea
Episodes last 3-7 days and recur about every 3 weeks, consistent with a periodic autoinflammatory syndrome pattern
Tender erythematous trunk-predominant rash with biopsy showing perivascular neutrophilic infiltrate without leukocytoclastic vasculitis
Lack of response to antihistamines, oral prednisone, and omalizumab argues against typical chronic urticaria/allergic etiologies
Extensive autoimmune/infectious/endocrine/GI workup (including colonoscopy) unrevealing, supporting an autoinflammatory periodic fever syndrome

Clinical signs of lepromatous leprosy/diffuse infiltration: cachexia, facial/ear infiltration with ciliary/supraciliary madarosis and nasal septal perforation

PMC5845136.diagnosis: Idiopathic hypereosinophilic syndrome presenting with eosinophilic myocarditis (Löffler myocarditis)
rationale_summary: Marked hypereosinophilia (AEC 21.6×10^3/μL; 63.5%) with systemic symptoms and hepatosplenomegaly

Acute cardiac involvement: progressive dyspnea/heart failure signs, elevated troponin I (35.4 ng/dL) and BNP (1280.5 pg/mL), ECG ST depressions, echo showing global LV hypokinesia with EF 40% and small pericardial effusion
Extensive evaluation negative for secondary eosinophilia (parasitic, viral, TB/HIV, blood cultures) and autoimmune disease (ANA/dsDNA negative)
Bone marrow: marked eosinophilia without lymphoproliferative infiltration; FIP1L1-PDGFRA and BCR-ABL negative, supporting idiopathic HES rather than a myeloid neoplasm

rationale_summary: Teen with long-standing migraine with aura developed new focal brainstem signs (diplopia with right internuclear ophthalmoplegia and gait imbalance) during a migraine-like attack

MRI showed a focal diffusion-restricting lesion with T2/FLAIR hyperintensity in the paramedian right dorsal midbrain involving the ipsilateral medial longitudinal fasciculus, consistent with an acute infarct

PMC11299065.diagnosis: Tropheryma whipplei (Whipple) culture-negative endocarditis causing severe aortic and mitral regurgitation with cardiogenic shock
rationale_summary: Known Whipple disease recently confirmed on intestinal biopsy
Severe new valvular disease on echocardiography (severe aortic and mitral regurgitation) with cardiogenic shock and anasarca
Marked pulmonary hypertension and right-heart failure on hemodynamics (RA 32 mm Hg, mPAP 55, PCWP 27, PVR 10 WU) consistent with advanced left-sided valvular failure
Blood Whipple PCR negative and right-ventricular endomyocardial biopsy negative do not exclude Whipple involvement localized to valves (typical for Whipple endocarditis)

PMC6961360.diagnosis: Early aortic bioprosthetic valve regurgitation due to stent post-sewing ring dehiscence with adjacent paravalvular leak
rationale_summary: After CPB, TEE showed a well-seated stented bovine pericardial valve with good leaflet mobility, arguing against gross malposition or leaflet immobility
Color Doppler demonstrated a perpendicular turbulent jet originating between the stent post and sewing ring (transprosthetic leak at the stent post-cuff interface)
Short-axis imaging showed flow convergence originating from outside the sewing ring adjacent to the stent post and a second jet arising from outside the sewing ring (paravalvular leak)
Both jets were in close proximity near the stent post, consistent with local cuff/stent-post disruption plus adjacent perivalvular leakage

PMC8436276.diagnosis: Pheochromocytoma-associated takotsubo syndrome (stress cardiomyopathy), midventricular variant precipitated perioperatively
rationale_summary: Catecholamine-producing adrenal tumor with hypertensive spells and elevated urinary metanephrines/normetanephrines
Acute postoperative shock with new LBBB and severe LV systolic dysfunction immediately after tumor removal
Coronary angiography showed no obstructive coronary disease or vasospasm, arguing against acute MI
Ventriculography/echocardiography showed circumferential midventricular (with apical) akinesia and reduced LVEF (25%), a takotsubo pattern
Marked troponin and NT-proBNP rise consistent with stress cardiomyopathy in this context

PMC9721027.diagnosis: Sigmoid volvulus causing closed-loop large-bowel obstruction with cecal perforation and generalized peritonitis
rationale_summary: Elderly man with acute large-bowel obstruction symptoms: progressive distension, persistent vomiting, constipation/absolute constipation, empty rectum
Initial pain in left iliac fossa with prior similar left-sided episode supports a distal (sigmoid) colonic pathology with intermittent torsion
Imaging shows markedly dilated colon with cecal diameter ≥12 cm consistent with distal colonic obstruction/closed-loop physiology

Free subdiaphragmatic air plus guarding, absent bowel sounds, fever, marked leukocytosis/CRP indicate perforation with peritonitis, most consistent with cecal blowout proximal to a left-sided obstruction such as sigmoid volvulus

PMC6831833.diagnosis: Sai-rei-to (herbal medicine)-induced diffuse alveolar hemorrhage / drug-induced lung injury
rationale_summary: Respiratory symptoms (dry cough, dyspnea, fever) began ~2 weeks after starting Sai-rei-to, with no other medications reported
Imaging showed new bilateral patchy ground-glass opacities and infiltrative shadows with pleural effusions

No prior interstitial lung disease and no clear alternative cause identified after evaluation for infection and cardiac failure (normal LV systolic function on echocardiography; bacteriological tests negative)

PMC6243328.diagnosis: Papillary tumor of the pineal region (PTPR)
rationale_summary: 25-year-old with a well-circumscribed pineal region mass causing obstructive hydrocephalus
Imaging shows T1 isointensity, FLAIR hyperintensity, and heterogeneous gadolinium enhancement with a small nonenhancing component
No calcification on CT, which is compatible with PTPR relative to many other pineal tumors
Mean ADC is elevated (facilitated diffusion), arguing against highly cellular lesions such as pineoblastoma/lymphoma
No spinal drop metastases, supporting a less aggressive pineal neoplasm

PMC4422990.diagnosis: Extrauterine low-grade endometrial stromal sarcoma (sigmoid colon) with nodal metastasis
rationale_summary: Sigmoid-colon mass composed of uniform small bland cells in sheets/nests infiltrating muscular wall and mesocolic fat, with prominent small arterioles (spiral arteriole-like pattern).
Immunophenotype strongly supports endometrial stromal differentiation: diffuse CD10+, estrogen receptor+, progesterone receptor+.
Excludes leading alternative (GIST) and other mimics: negative c-kit, DOG1, CD34, SMA, desmin, S100, and pan-cytokeratin.
Shows malignant behavior despite bland cytology: extensive lymphovascular invasion and regional lymph node metastases with low Ki-67 (8%), consistent with low-grade ESS.

PMC7455415.diagnosis: Micronodular thymoma with lymphoid stroma (anterior mediastinal thymoma) mistaken for an ectopic parathyroid adenoma
rationale_summary: CT showed an 18-mm anterior mediastinal mass posterior to the sternum, a typical thymic location
Frozen section described a nodular epithelial neoplasm with peripheral lymphocytes and occasional pseudoglandular structures, fitting thymic epithelial tumor morphology
Intraoperative PTH did not fall after mass removal (149.3 pg/mL at 10 min, rising to 259.5 pg/mL at 30 min), arguing the resected lesion was not the hyperfunctioning parathyroid source

PMC6188590.diagnosis: Statin-associated immune-mediated necrotizing myopathy (anti-HMG-CoA reductase antibody-mediated)
rationale_summary: Long-term statin exposure (atorvastatin for 6 years) followed by proximal myalgias/subjective weakness with rising CPK (to 720 U/L)
Recurrence of proximal myalgias with a different statin (pravastatin) and elevated CPK (586 U/L) and ESR 51 mm/hr
CPK continued to rise markedly (to 1400 U/L) despite statin discontinuation, supporting an immune-mediated process rather than simple toxic statin myopathy
Symptoms and CPK initially improved with prednisone and statin cessation, consistent with steroid-responsive inflammatory/autoimmune myopathy

Respiratory symptoms began within days of starting vildagliptin and progressed over 2 weeks
CT showed extensive ground-glass opacities with irregular reticulation in a peribronchovascular, basal-dominant distribution with restrictive physiology (FVC 43.2%)
BAL showed lymphocytosis with eosinophilia (57% lymphocytes, 12% eosinophils) and microbiology was negative
Very high IgE and markedly elevated KL-6 support inflammatory interstitial lung disease
Biopsy showed organizing pneumonia with acute alveolar injury pattern and eosinophilic/lymphoplasmacytic infiltrates, consistent with drug-induced interstitial pneumonia
Autoimmune serologies were negative and there were no connective-tissue disease signs, supporting a drug reaction

PMC3914239.diagnosis: Intrathoracic ectopic left kidney with left renal artery stenosis causing secondary (malignant) hypertension and advanced chronic kidney disease

PMC6787862.diagnosis: Primary adrenal insufficiency (Addison disease) presenting with adrenal crisis-related renal salt wasting and severe hypotonic hypovolemic hyponatremia
rationale_summary: Severe hypotonic hyponatremia (Na 101, plasma osmolality 225) with clinical hypovolemia/orthostasis
Inappropriately high urine sodium (128) despite hypovolemia, consistent with renal salt wasting from mineralocorticoid deficiency
Hyperkalemia (K 5.2) and metabolic acidosis (CO2 16) support aldosterone deficiency
Systemic symptoms and hyperpigmentation ("tanning") suggest chronic primary adrenal insufficiency
History of autoimmune hypothyroidism increases likelihood of autoimmune Addison disease

PMC2958974.diagnosis: Sepsis-induced cardiomyopathy due to postpartum Escherichia coli urosepsis (pyelonephritis)
rationale_summary: 12 hours postpartum developed fever (40°C), chills, right costovertebral tenderness with pyuria/hematuria and leukocytosis consistent with acute pyelonephritis
Blood and urine cultures grew Escherichia coli, indicating systemic infection
Rapid progression to respiratory failure with bilateral pleural effusions and no pulmonary embolus on CT suggests severe sepsis-related lung injury

Echocardiogram showed new biventricular hypokinesis with reduced EF (35%) but normal end-diastolic chamber size and normal valves, supporting acute septic myocardial depression rather than dilated peripartum cardiomyopathy
Normal troponin and ECG without ischemic changes argue against acute coronary syndrome as the cause of low EF

PMC8082195.diagnosis: Immune-mediated thrombotic thrombocytopenic purpura (TTP) with central nervous system involvement
rationale_summary: Acute waxing/waning altered mental status with transient focal neurologic symptoms
Severe thrombocytopenia (7,000/mcL) plus hemolytic anemia evidence (hemoglobin drop, elevated LDH, very low haptoglobin)
Minimal renal involvement (creatinine 1.0 mg/dL) consistent with TTP spectrum
Brain MRI shows multifocal patchy T2/FLAIR lesions with punctate susceptibility foci (microhemorrhages), compatible with microvascular thrombotic injury seen in TTP

CSF essentially normal and extensive viral testing negative, arguing against viral encephalitis and supporting a thrombotic microangiopathy process treated with plasmapheresis/steroids; ADAMTS13 assay sent

PMC5015421.diagnosis: Common peroneal neuropathy at the fibular neck due to compression from a thromboembolic deterrent stocking
rationale_summary: New painless high-stepping foot drop 3 weeks after surgery with weakness of dorsiflexion/eversion and toe extension (1/5) and relatively spared inversion (4/5), localizing to the common peroneal nerve

Sensory loss over lateral lower leg and dorsum of foot including first webspace, with preserved plantar sensation and ankle reflexes, supporting peroneal nerve involvement rather than tibial nerve/root pathology
Well-demarcated indentation at the proximal rim of the stocking at the fibular neck on the affected side suggests focal external compression at the typical site of peroneal entrapment

PMC8811182.diagnosis: Delayed migration/extrusion of a silicone orbital implant with secondary orbital pyogranulomatous inflammation mimicking a retained prosthesis
rationale_summary: Two months after enucleation with a silicone orbital implant, the left orbit developed swelling and a draining tract/open wound
A silicone implant was recovered from vomitus, indicating the implant had migrated from its intended orbital location

Despite apparent continued palpation of a "sphere," surgical exploration revealed a firm pinkish-white mass occupying the orbit without gross foreign material, consistent with inflammatory/granulation tissue replacing the implant

PMC8828200.diagnosis: Chronic enteropathy associated with SLCO2A1 (CEAS; chronic nonspecific multiple ulcers of the small intestine)

Biopsies showed only nonspecific lymphocytic infiltration without malignancy or cytomegalovirus
Lesions persisted unchanged on repeat colonoscopy despite discontinuation of NSAIDs, arguing against NSAID enteropathy
Infectious and systemic/vasculitic causes were not supported (negative stool cultures, negative MPO/PR3-ANCA/ANA, unremarkable contrast CT)

PMC4898367.diagnosis: Primary sebaceous carcinoma of the parotid gland
rationale_summary: Parotid mass with cervical lymphadenopathy and biopsy showing poorly differentiated carcinoma
Histology shows lobulated nests of large foamy cells with vacuolated clear cytoplasm and central nuclei, surrounded by basaloid cells-features of sebaceous differentiation
Areas of squamous islands with keratin pearl formation support carcinoma with sebaceous/squamous differentiation
PAS negative foamy cells argues against mucin-rich tumors (eg, mucoepidermoid carcinoma)

Immunoprofile supports epithelial carcinoma (EMA and pancytokeratin positive) with p63 expression; negative S100, CD10, vimentin, melan A, and CD45 helps exclude myoepithelial, renal, mesenchymal, melanocytic, or lymphoid neoplasms

PMC10765237.diagnosis: Lepromatous leprosy (multibacillary Hansen disease, possibly histoid variant)

On the fifth course she developed persistent cough, crackles, and progressive bilateral nonsegmental subpleural consolidations on CT
Peripheral eosinophilia (736/µL) with elevated interstitial lung markers (SP-D 250 U/L, KL-6 538 U/mL) supports eosinophilic/drug-induced pneumonitis
No response and worsening despite 5 days of broad-spectrum antibiotics and negative urine antigens argues against typical bacterial pneumonia

rationale_summary: Acute onset fingertip pain, paresthesia, color change progressing to refractory digital ulcerations suggests severe secondary Raynaud/digital ischemia
Systemic symptoms (malaise, fatigue, night sweats, alopecia, dry eyes) developed over months with negative autoimmune/inflammatory laboratory evaluation

CT chest/abdomen/pelvis showed peritoneal carcinomatosis with omental caking and markedly elevated CA-125 (862 U/mL) with normal CEA/CA19-9, supporting ovarian/peritoneal serous carcinoma as the underlying malignancy

PMC9446685.diagnosis: Transient constrictive pericarditis after cardiac surgery (post-pericardiotomy constriction)
rationale_summary: Presented 4 weeks post-CABG/bioprosthetic AVR with predominantly right-sided heart failure symptoms/signs (marked JVP with Kussmaul's sign, progressive oedema, pleural effusions)

Echocardiography showed constrictive physiology: thickened bright pericardium, dilated IVC without respirophasic collapse, septal bounce (ventricular interdependence), and marked respiratory variation in mitral (>25%) and tricuspid (>40%) inflow velocities
LV systolic function and prosthetic valve function were normal, arguing against primary myocardial or valvular causes of decompensation

PMC5953188.diagnosis: Congenital pulmonary airway malformation (CPAM), type 1 (right lung) with secondary left lung hypoplasia
rationale_summary: Prenatal ultrasound showed a large cystic mass occupying the right hemithorax with reduced left thoracic volume
Autopsy: markedly enlarged, hyperinflated right lung with multiple contiguous cystic spaces up to 7 cm and mediastinal shift; small immature left lung

Histology of right lung: cysts formed by proliferation of terminal respiratory structures lined by ciliated pseudostratified epithelium with mucinous/goblet cells and mucinous glands, consistent with CPAM type 1

PMC4405311.diagnosis: Cardiac tamponade from massive pericardial effusion due to severe hypothyroidism (myxedema)
rationale_summary: Clinical tamponade physiology: hypotension (60/30 mmHg), raised JVP (11 cm H2O), very distant heart sounds, pulsus paradoxus, impalpable apex beat, low pulse volume, severe dyspnea
Imaging showed enlarged cardiac silhouette with large pericardial effusion and bilateral pleural effusions
Effusions were transudative and there were generalized edema/ascites, supporting a systemic cause such as hypothyroidism rather than infection (Mantoux negative, no fever)
Associated anemia (Hb 7.6 g/dL) is compatible with hypothyroidism

PMC9857465.diagnosis: Erdheim-Chester disease (non-Langerhans cell histiocytosis) with biliary involvement mimicking sclerosing cholangitis
rationale_summary: Marked cholestatic liver enzymes with MRCP/ERCP showing multifocal intrahepatic changes and later a dominant common hepatic duct/hilar stricture with negative cytology/biopsy
Abdominal CT showed bilateral perinephric fat stranding and diffuse large-vessel inflammation, a characteristic pattern for Erdheim-Chester disease
Lower-extremity radiographs demonstrated diffuse sclerotic changes of long bones, typical of Erdheim-Chester disease
Development of dome-shaped cutaneous lesions later in the course supports a systemic infiltrative/histiocytic disorder rather than primary sclerosing cholangitis
Workup for alternative causes (viral/autoimmune cholangitis, IgG4-related disease) was unremarkable, making a systemic infiltrative process more likely

PMC10023862.diagnosis: Multiple hereditary infundibulocystic basal cell carcinoma syndrome (a basal cell nevus syndrome variant)
rationale_summary: Numerous small (2-6 mm) pearly papules on the face and in linear strings along upper/lower eyelid margins for years, clinically and dermoscopically consistent with basal cell carcinoma (arborizing vessels, brown-gray globules)
Multiple biopsied facial papules all showed infundibulocystic basal cell carcinoma with follicular differentiation
History of numerous basal cell carcinomas since 2015

Lack of other classic Gorlin findings (no palmar pits, macrocephaly, jawline deformities, or skeletal abnormalities) supports an infundibulocystic/milder variant presenting predominantly with multiple BCCs

PMC7924147.diagnosis: Disseminated histoplasmosis (Histoplasma capsulatum) with oral/maxillary involvement causing primary adrenal insufficiency
rationale_summary: Advanced untreated HIV (CD4 27 cells/µL) predisposes to disseminated fungal infection
Biopsy of the maxillary mass showed granulomatous tissue with macrophages containing well-demarcated fungal spores consistent with Histoplasma yeasts
Systemic dissemination suggested by hilar lymphadenopathy, maculopapular rash, and bilateral adrenal enlargement
Primary adrenal insufficiency supported by hyponatremia, hyperkalemia, elevated ACTH, and inadequate cosyntropin response with negative 21-hydroxylase antibodies

PMC3324215.diagnosis: Right ureteropelvic junction (UPJ) obstruction due to a crossing accessory renal vessel, resulting in severe hydronephrosis with a nonfunctioning right kidney
rationale_summary: Marked right hydronephrosis with calyceal dilatation and parenchymal thinning while the right ureter is not dilated, localizing obstruction to the UPJ
Doppler ultrasound shows an accessory vessel crossing the pyeloureteral junction, consistent with extrinsic UPJ compression
No right-sided excretion on intravenous urography and no right renal uptake on 99Tc-DMSA indicate a nonfunctioning obstructed kidney
Left kidney imaging and function are preserved, supporting unilateral UPJ obstruction rather than systemic renal disease

PMC11057788.diagnosis: Restrictive cardiomyopathy (restrictive physiology of the cardiac allograft)

Biopsies negative for rejection and coronary angiography without obstructive disease, supporting a nonischemic restrictive process

PMC5005770.diagnosis: Chronic mefloquine-induced neuropsychiatric toxicity with central vestibulopathy
rationale_summary: Symptoms began 2 weeks after starting mefloquine prophylaxis (anxiety, vivid dreams) and progressed while he continued the drug for 6 months (confusion, disequilibrium).

Persistent long-term neuropsychiatric and sleep symptoms (emotional lability, concentration/memory and word-finding difficulty, insomnia with vivid dreams/nightmares) plus chronic dizziness/"sea legs," frequent vertigo and falls.
Vestibular testing showed hyperactive VOR gains and low phase on rotary chair testing, consistent with a central vestibulopathy, with normal brain/IAC MRI and negative infectious/toxicology workup.
Reported combat stressor did not change symptoms, and psychiatric/PTSD-directed treatments improved mood only partially while vestibular and sleep symptoms persisted.

PMC10065398.diagnosis: Subacute (de Quervain) thyroiditis presenting as unilateral thyroiditis
rationale_summary: Two-week anterior neck/supraclavicular pain worsened by swallowing after an upper respiratory infection with enlarged tender right thyroid lobe
Thyrotoxicosis with suppressed TSH and elevated free T4/T3 plus markedly elevated thyroglobulin, consistent with destructive thyroiditis
Inflammatory markers elevated (CRP 38.5 mg/L, mild leukocytosis) with negative thyroid antibodies
Ultrasound shows enlarged right lobe with inhomogeneous hypoechogenicity and decreased vascularity with normal left lobe, typical for subacute thyroiditis
Rapid symptomatic improvement after starting high-dose prednisolone supports subacute thyroiditis

PMC2783065.diagnosis: Superior mesenteric artery (SMA) syndrome causing duodenal obstruction from rapid weight loss/anorexia nervosa
rationale_summary: Severe weight loss (22 kg), BMI 15, amenorrhea and bradycardia consistent with malnutrition
Progressive postprandial epigastric pain relieved by vomiting with evolution to bilious emesis suggests proximal intestinal obstruction distal to the ampulla
Barium study showed massively dilated stomach with failure of contrast to pass beyond the third portion of the duodenum
CT demonstrated narrowing of the third portion of the duodenum compressed between the SMA and aorta, diagnostic of SMA syndrome

PMC8853404.diagnosis: COVID-19-associated mononeuritis multiplex (acute axonal tibial and common peroneal mononeuropathies)
rationale_summary: New focal left foot drop after recent critical COVID-19 treated with high-dose steroids and tocilizumab
Exam showed distal-predominant weakness (left ankle dorsiflexion 2/5) with absent knee/ankle reflexes and otherwise normal neurologic exam, favoring peripheral nerve involvement

NCS/EMG demonstrated acute axonal mononeuropathies involving multiple individual nerves (bilateral tibial mononeuropathies and severe bilateral common peroneal mononeuropathies) with upper extremities spared
Findings were localized to noncompressible sites and involved multiple nerves rather than a length-dependent polyneuropathy or radiculopathy

PMC10442471.diagnosis: Adult-onset Still's disease (systemic inflammatory disorder causing serositis)
rationale_summary: Persistent quotidian high fevers (39-40 °C) with negative blood/urine/pleural cultures and unrevealing infectious/oncologic workup
Marked systemic inflammation with neutrophilic leukocytosis, very high CRP/ESR, and hyperferritinemia (ferritin 3254 ng/mL)
Cytopenia pattern and thrombocytosis with hepatosplenomegaly and FDG-avid spleen/bone marrow/lymph nodes consistent with systemic inflammatory disease rather than focal infection
Exudative recurrent pleural effusions and pleural biopsy showing chronic nonspecific pleuritis (serositis) with negative malignancy/TB studies
Autoimmune and vasculitis panels negative, supporting a seronegative autoinflammatory diagnosis

PMC6389478.diagnosis: Left pyriform sinus fistula due to a third/fourth branchial cleft (pouch) anomaly
rationale_summary: Recurrent left-sided cervical abscesses since childhood despite multiple drainages/operations and repeatedly negative standard imaging for a second-arch lesion
Episodes almost invariably follow upper respiratory tract infections, consistent with an internal pharyngeal opening acting as a conduit for infection

Original "second branchial cleft cyst" pathology showed pseudoepithelium rather than true cyst epithelium, arguing against a true second-arch cyst and supporting an occult branchial pouch sinus/fistula

PMC10860510.diagnosis: Idiopathic myointimal hyperplasia of the mesenteric veins (IMHMV) causing left-sided ischemic colitis
rationale_summary: Middle-aged man with obstructive/colitis symptoms and sigmoid luminal narrowing with marked mucosal edema on colonoscopy, initially mis-suspected as IBD/eosinophilic enteritis but biopsy showed only mild nonspecific inflammation

Contrast CT showed long-segment left-sided colonic wall thickening (splenic flexure to rectum) with a thin, cord-like inferior mesenteric vein and ectatic mesenteric venous branches while the inferior mesenteric artery was patent
No arterial disease or venous thrombus/calcifications, supporting a nonthrombotic mesenteric venous occlusive process characteristic of IMHMV

PMC11101242.diagnosis: Delusional infestation (somatic-type delusional disorder; Morgellons-like ocular presentation)
rationale_summary: 15-year fixed belief of persistent foreign material in the superior fornices with repeated self-extraction/collection rituals (sink filling/draining, mascara brush)
Multiple ophthalmologists repeatedly everted lids and swept fornices with no retained foreign body; repeated exams in 2019 and 2023 again showed no lesions/foreign bodies
Orbital radiograph and MRI were normal despite ongoing reports of diverse "debris" (plant parts, fibers, hair, metal filaments, insects)
Ocular findings limited to mild surface disease (trace injection, punctate staining) that does not explain the extensive foreign-body narrative

PMC2892661.diagnosis: Cervical spondylotic myeloradiculopathy (compressive cervical myelopathy mimicking ALS)
rationale_summary: Progressive upper-limb weakness with atrophy/fasciculations plus extremely brisk reflexes suggests combined LMN and UMN involvement
Neck pain, arm numbness/clumsiness, hyper-/dysesthesia over shoulders/proximal arms, and reduced vibration sense indicate sensory pathway involvement not typical for ALS
Gait ataxia with preserved bulbar function and flexor plantar responses fits a cervical spinal cord/nerve root process rather than primary motor neuron disease

PMC3638553.diagnosis: Chronic inflammatory demyelinating polyradiculoneuropathy (CIDP) causing respiratory muscle weakness with chronic hypercapnic respiratory failure
rationale_summary: Three months of progressive symmetric proximal and distal limb weakness with distal sensory loss and hyporeflexia suggests a demyelinating polyneuropathy
Arterial blood gas shows chronic hypoventilation (pCO2 58 mm Hg) with hypoxemia despite clear lungs and no evidence of heart failure on BNP/echocardiogram
Lack of improvement with diuretics and persistence of abnormal ABGs despite glycemic control supports a neuromuscular rather than cardiopulmonary or metabolic cause of dyspnea

PMC9005318.diagnosis: Acute severe aortic regurgitation due to paravalvular leak after transcatheter aortic valve replacement (TAVR)
rationale_summary: Symptoms began 3 days after transfemoral TAVR with new rapid-onset exertional dyspnea and orthopnea
Exam shows a grade 3 diastolic decrescendo murmur at the left upper sternal border with wide pulse pressure and warm extremities, consistent with significant aortic regurgitation

Findings of central congestion, pulmonary congestion on imaging, and markedly elevated pro-BNP indicate acute decompensated heart failure without alternative causes on CT (no PE, dissection, or pericardial effusion)

PMC6864292.diagnosis: Idiopathic hypereosinophilic syndrome (HES) with thrombotic/vasculopathic cutaneous necrosis and pulmonary involvement
rationale_summary: Marked persistent hypereosinophilia (21.15×10^9/L; 57.8%) with bone marrow eosinophilia (27.5%)
Secondary causes of eosinophilia (allergic, parasitic, rheumatologic, immunologic) were unremarkable

End-organ manifestations compatible with HES: large progressive cutaneous necrosis with biopsy showing dermal necrosis/hemorrhage and eosinophil-rich perivascular inflammation, plus pulmonary symptoms (chest pain, hemoptysis) with bilateral opacities/pleural effusion

Prominent prothrombotic laboratory profile (very high D-dimer 82.20 mg/L FEU, thrombocytopenia) consistent with eosinophilia-associated thrombosis/vasculopathy contributing to necrosis and lung findings

PMC9449604.diagnosis: Pembrolizumab-induced immune-related pancreatitis (immune checkpoint inhibitor-associated pancreatitis with mass-forming/autoimmune pancreatitis-like features)
rationale_summary: History of prolonged pembrolizumab therapy with new pancreatic abnormalities appearing 3 months after discontinuation

Imaging shows focal pancreatic head enlargement with delayed enhancement, diffusion restriction, and multifocal main pancreatic duct stenoses plus intrapancreatic bile duct stenosis-pattern compatible with inflammatory/immune-mediated pancreatitis mimicking a mass
No clinical/laboratory evidence of acute pancreatitis (normal amylase/lipase and CRP; asymptomatic) and serum IgG4 is normal, making classic IgG4-related autoimmune pancreatitis less likely
Multiple cytology samples (pancreatic juice, bile, bile-duct brushings) are negative for malignancy, supporting a non-neoplastic process in the setting of immune checkpoint inhibitor exposure

PMC1971059.diagnosis: Primary skeletal muscle carcinoid tumor (well-differentiated neuroendocrine tumor) of the thigh
rationale_summary: Slowly enlarging firm intramuscular thigh mass (sartorius) excised en bloc
Histology with polygonal cells in packets separated by fine fibrovascular stroma, consistent with a neuroendocrine neoplasm
Immunoprofile: strong synaptophysin/chromogranin/NSE positivity with cytokeratin positivity; negative S100 (argues against paraganglioma) and desmin (not myogenic)
Electron microscopy shows abundant neurosecretory granules
Elevated urinary 5-HIAA supports serotonin-producing carcinoid; imaging (CT, MIBG, octreotide) shows no extra-site disease suggesting a primary lesion in muscle

PMC11658845.diagnosis: Neonatal severe primary hyperparathyroidism (most consistent with inactivating calcium-sensing receptor [CaSR] mutation)
rationale_summary: Severe neonatal hypercalcemia (total Ca 14.3 mg/dL, ionized Ca 2.32 mmol/L) with profoundly elevated PTH (2116 pg/mL), indicating PTH-dependent hypercalcemia
Generalized osteopenia/demineralization with metaphyseal lucencies and bowing/healing fractures, consistent with marked PTH-mediated bone resorption
Early presentation with respiratory distress and bell-shaped chest, typical of severe neonatal hyperparathyroidism affecting the thoracic cage
Normal 25-hydroxyvitamin D level argues against vitamin D excess as the cause of hypercalcemia

PMC4356045.diagnosis: Unilateral giant bullous emphysema (vanishing lung syndrome) of the left lung
rationale_summary: Chest radiograph showed marked hyperlucency of the upper half of the left hemithorax and CT demonstrated multiple bullae occupying almost the entire left hemithorax with only limited residual lung/bronchiectatic changes.

Pulmonary function showed marked gas trapping (residual volume 156% predicted) and a large discrepancy between TLC by nitrogen washout (56% predicted) and plethysmography (88% predicted), consistent with poorly communicating bullous spaces.
Workup for alternative causes was unrevealing (normal α1-antitrypsin, immunoglobulins, connective tissue disease screen; bronchoscopy/BAL/biopsies negative for infection or malignancy).

PMC10509802.diagnosis: Acute intratumoral hemorrhage into a cervicofacial plexiform neurofibroma (NF1) causing rapidly expanding hematoma
rationale_summary: Known NF1 stigmata (prior tongue neurofibroma, multiple café-au-lait spots, congenital lateral neck mass).

CT angiography showed a large ill-defined lobulated suprahyoid neck lesion infiltrating multiple spaces and encasing the jugular vein and internal carotid artery, consistent with plexiform neurofibroma.
After minor head trauma, the unilateral temporoparietal/periorbital swelling rapidly enlarged over hours with tense skin and punctate hemorrhagic spots, indicating acute hemorrhagic expansion.
No intracranial injury or ruptured aneurysm on imaging, supporting hemorrhage within/around the NF1-associated lesion rather than intracranial bleeding.

No axillary lymphadenopathy or systemic symptoms despite locally infiltrative clinical appearance

PMC7374193.diagnosis: Bilateral optic nerve head hemangioblastomas (retinal capillary hemangioblastomas, VHL-like but genetically negative)
rationale_summary: 15-year history of bilateral optic nerve head swelling with suspected optic disc masses initially misattributed to pseudotumor cerebri and unresponsive to optic nerve sheath fenestration
Progressive bilateral optic nerve head lesions extending into the papillomacular bundles with severe bilateral vision loss (20/20→20/400 OD; 20/150→NLP OS)
Chronic use of intravitreal anti-VEGF agents and steroid implants with limited response is consistent with attempting to manage exudative/vascular optic disc tumors
Work-up including whole exome sequencing negative for VHL and other syndromic causes, supporting sporadic optic disc hemangioblastoma phenotype

PMC8462061.diagnosis: Insulin autoimmune syndrome (autoimmune hyperinsulinemic hypoglycemia due to insulin autoantibodies) occurring with evolving type 1 diabetes
rationale_summary: Recurrent fasting hypoglycemia with inappropriately elevated insulin (158.35 pmol/L) and C-peptide (1.09 nmol/L) plus suppressed beta-hydroxybutyrate indicates endogenous hyperinsulinism

PMC11066590.diagnosis: Lorlatinib-induced bilateral optic neuritis (toxic optic neuropathy) with associated reversible splenial lesion
rationale_summary: Started lorlatinib 100 mg daily 2 months before onset of symptoms
Developed painless, progressive bilateral vision loss to no light perception
Exam showed large minimally reactive pupils with left RAPD and trace optic disc edema (L>R)
Orbital MRI showed bilateral optic nerve T2 hyperintensity and postcontrast enhancement extending to the chiasm
Brain MRI showed an isolated splenial corpus callosum diffusion-restricting/FLAIR lesion without other abnormalities, supporting a medication-related neurotoxicity pattern

PMC2547113.diagnosis: Nutritional optic neuropathy due to folate deficiency (tobacco-alcohol amblyopia)
rationale_summary: Progressive, painless bilateral visual loss with bilateral central scotomas and severe dyschromatopsia (unable to identify Ishihara plates)
Normal-appearing optic discs (no pallor) and normal peripheral visual fields, consistent with early optic neuropathy affecting central vision
Marked folate deficiency (low serum and red cell folate) with macrocytosis (MCV 122.8 fl) despite normal hemoglobin
Poor diet with minimal leafy vegetables plus smoking (20/day) and regular alcohol intake, risk factors for nutritional/toxic optic neuropathy

PMC4074175.diagnosis: Pheochromocytoma presenting with refractory anxiety/panic-like symptoms
rationale_summary: Late-onset, progressively severe anxiety with prominent autonomic symptoms (palpitations, occasional numbness)
Marked diurnal/cyclic pattern with pronounced early-morning (dawn) worsening and evening relief suggests episodic catecholamine surges rather than primary anxiety disorder
History of hypertension increases suspicion for a catecholamine-secreting tumor
Persistent symptoms despite multiple adequate SSRI trials and mirtazapine argues against typical primary anxiety/OCD
Chronic sensation of "chest heat" is consistent with adrenergic episodes/flushing reported in catecholamine excess

PMC5471795.diagnosis: Idiopathic uveal effusion syndrome (unilateral ciliochoroidal effusion with exudative retinal detachment)
rationale_summary: Unilateral large serous (exudative) neurosensory retinal detachment involving macular/peripapillary regions on OCT and fundus exam
Affected eye shows clinically shallow anterior chamber and a myopic shift compared with fellow eye, consistent with anterior displacement from uveal/ciliochoroidal effusion
Minimal intraocular inflammation (no anterior chamber inflammation; only +1 anterior vitreous cells) and normal IOP, making VKH/uveitis less likely
Biometry shows the affected eye has a shallower anterior chamber depth and slightly shorter axial length, supporting a uveal-effusion mechanism

PMC6587389.diagnosis: Giant cell arteritis (temporal arteritis) causing ischemic tongue necrosis
rationale_summary: Elderly woman with acute painful, well-demarcated anterior tongue necrosis preceded by blue discoloration (ischemia) and odynophagia
Marked inflammatory markers and reactive cytoses (ESR 65 mm/h, platelets 716,000/µL, leukocytes 22,000/µL) compatible with large-vessel vasculitis
CT angiogram showing undulated/beaded distal internal carotid and vertebral arteries bilaterally supports large-vessel vasculitis
Infectious causes were not supported: no response to antibiotics/antivirals/antifungal therapy and tongue biopsy negative for fungal elements and CMV/HSV/VZV

PMC7901359.diagnosis: Cerebral salt wasting syndrome (CSW) causing hypovolemic hypotonic hyponatremia after head trauma
rationale_summary: Severe hypotonic hyponatremia (Na 118 meq/L, serum osmolality 226 mosm/kg)
Clinical hypovolemia: dry mucous membranes, reduced skin turgor, hypotension, marked orthostatic drop with dizziness; no edema/JVD
Inappropriately high renal sodium loss and concentrated urine despite hyponatremia (urine Na 83 meq/L, urine osmolality 518 mosm/kg)
Low serum uric acid (2.6 mg/dL), supporting natriuretic hyponatremia pattern
Recent CNS injury with subsequent intracranial findings (prior skull fracture; new right frontal hygroma) consistent with CSW trigger

PMC6029059.diagnosis: Hepatic veno-occlusive disease (sinusoidal obstruction syndrome) after autologous HSCT
rationale_summary: Occurs shortly after BEAM conditioning and autologous stem-cell transplantation (Day +8 to +9)
Classic findings: rapid weight gain >5%, hepatomegaly, ascites/fluid retention, oligoanuria
Marked acute hepatocellular injury with rising AST/ALT and hyperbilirubinemia, with progressively increasing INR
Ultrasound shows decreased portal-venous flow velocity consistent with hepatic outflow obstruction
Refractory thrombocytopenia and elevated PAI-1 support endothelial injury/VOD in this context

PMC7904273.diagnosis: Celiac disease (gluten-sensitive enteropathy) complicated by aplastic/hypocellular marrow-associated cytopenias
rationale_summary: 6 months of chronic diarrhea with intermittent abdominal pain, fatigue, and mild weight z-score decline
Evidence of intestinal inflammation/malabsorption: hypoalbuminemia (2.8 g/dL), positive fecal occult blood, elevated ESR/CRP; infectious stool studies negative
Endoscopy showed duodenal mucosal congestion/edema (typical site for celiac involvement); tTG IgA and duodenal biopsies are being pursued

Severe neutropenia with anemia and low reticulocytes plus mild thrombocytopenia, with bone marrow biopsy showing hypocellularity and no malignancy or nutrient deficiencies, supports aplastic/hypocellular marrow rather than deficiency or infiltration, consistent with an autoimmune association such as celiac disease

parsed_output.diagnosis: Acute myocarditis (likely viral)
rationale_summary: Recent viral prodrome followed by recurrent sharp central chest pain
Dynamic troponin T rise (17→33 ng/L in 2 hours, later 731 ng/L) despite no ischemic ECG changes
Normal transthoracic echocardiogram without regional wall-motion abnormalities
CT angiogram excluded aortic dissection and central pulmonary embolus, supporting a non-ischemic myocardial injury cause
Severe hypocalcemia (Ca 5.5 mg/dL) with hyperphosphatemia (P 7.6 mg/dL) and markedly elevated intact PTH (343.1 pg/mL) indicates end-organ resistance to PTH rather than deficiency
No features of Albright's hereditary osteodystrophy, supporting type 1B over type 1A

PMC4174964.diagnosis: Minocycline-induced drug-induced liver injury causing acute hepatic dysfunction
rationale_summary: Recent exposure to minocycline for acne with no other liver disease risk factors reported
Two-week onset of scleral icterus, abdominal distension (mild ascites), edema, and fatigue consistent with hepatic dysfunction
Labs show hyperbilirubinemia with only mild AST/ALT elevation but significant synthetic impairment (hypoalbuminemia, elevated INR)
Hemolysis evaluation normal, supporting hepatic (not hemolytic) cause of jaundice
Ultrasound only mild ascites and otherwise unremarkable, supporting a nonobstructive/intrinsic hepatic process

PMC11450870.diagnosis: Pneumocystis jirovecii pneumonia (PJP) after rituximab-related immunosuppression
rationale_summary: Developed fever, nonproductive cough, and significant respiratory distress shortly after rituximab while on tacrolimus, with pancytopenia/neutropenia (ANC 927) indicating immunosuppression

Imaging evolved from diffuse bilateral hazy opacities to HRCT showing bilateral multicystic lesions with surrounding consolidation and ground-glass attenuation-pattern consistent with PJP with cystic/pneumatocele changes

No response to broad-spectrum antibiotics, empiric antituberculous therapy, or fluconazole; repeated microbiologic tests (bacterial, fungal, TB) were negative, supporting a nonbacterial opportunistic pneumonia such as PJP

PMC11264972.diagnosis: Azathioprine hypersensitivity syndrome (drug-induced systemic reaction mimicking sepsis) with acute pustular eruption and myocardial injury
rationale_summary: Symptoms began shortly after starting azathioprine (fever, abdominal pain, hypotension) with no infectious source on imaging/cultures
Developed acute erythematous pustular rash during hospitalization
Marked recurrence within 12 hours of a single azathioprine re-challenge dose, with vasodilatory shock and decreased consciousness
Associated transient ST elevations and very high troponin with normal coronary angiography, supporting drug-related myocardial injury rather than obstructive CAD

PMC9296717.diagnosis: Postoperative abdominal wall myofascial pain syndrome due to a myofascial trigger point in the right internal oblique muscle
rationale_summary: Severe localized right upper abdominal wall pain with movement and a positive Carnett's sign supports an abdominal wall source
Exam showed a hypersensitive tender spot with muscle stiffness/taut band and palpation reproduced radiating epigastric pain, meeting trigger-point criteria
Pain improved after local anesthetic (mepivacaine) injection into the tender spot, consistent with trigger-point pain

Ultrasound showed a focal intramuscular hyperechoic spot with surrounding hypoechoic area within the internal oblique and preserved muscle fiber continuity, favoring a myofascial trigger point rather than port-site disruption or a vertical nerve lesion

PMC1808459.diagnosis: Fibromatosis-like spindle cell metaplastic carcinoma of the breast (low-grade spindle cell carcinoma)
rationale_summary: Infiltrative spindle cell tumor in breast with prominent keloid-like sclerosis and mild atypia/rare mitoses (low-grade appearance)

Diffuse epithelial marker expression in spindle cells (cytokeratins, CK7, high-molecular weight CK, EMA) with vimentin coexpression supports metaplastic carcinoma rather than benign fibromatosis or primary sarcoma
Focal p63 and smooth muscle actin positivity is compatible with metaplastic/myoepithelial differentiation
ER/PR/HER2 negative and low Ki-67 (<5%) fit low-grade metaplastic spindle cell carcinoma phenotype

ECG changes (ST depression, T-wave inversion) and paralytic ileus (distended abdomen, absent bowel sounds) are consistent with severe hypokalemia

ST changes and symptoms resolved within 10 minutes after immediate lead withdrawal and repositioning

PMC11128951.diagnosis: Intramuscular hemangioma (venous malformation) adjacent to the distal femur with reactive cortical thickening

PMC11524424.diagnosis: Myxedema coma (decompensated severe hypothyroidism from levothyroxine discontinuation)
rationale_summary: History of congenital hypothyroidism with months of levothyroxine nonadherence and severe malnutrition/weight loss

PMC6169071.diagnosis: Pseudo-thrombotic microangiopathy from severe vitamin B12 deficiency (megaloblastic anemia)
rationale_summary: Severe anemia with hemolysis markers (schistocytes ~10%, very high LDH, undetectable haptoglobin) and negative direct Coombs
Inappropriately low reticulocyte production index (0.061) despite profound anemia, suggesting ineffective erythropoiesis rather than true peripheral TMA
Bicytopenia/pancytopenia pattern: neutropenia on presentation with later thrombocytopenia, without blasts
Neurologic symptoms (finger paresthesia, headaches) compatible with B12 deficiency
Normal coagulation studies argue against DIC; overall picture fits B12-deficiency-related pseudo-TMA

PMC10191487.diagnosis: Congenital hepatic fibrosis with portal hypertension causing hypersplenism
rationale_summary: Splenomegaly beginning in infancy progressing to persistent hepatosplenomegaly
Pancytopenia with recurrent spontaneous normalization consistent with hypersplenism/sequestration rather than marrow failure
Liver ultrasound showing heterogeneous hyperechoic parenchyma and elevated hepatic arterial resistive index (0.8), supporting hepatic fibrosis/portal hypertension
Bone marrow biopsy with trilineage hematopoiesis and no leukemia/lymphoma; viral PCRs and lysosomal storage disease workup negative
Liver enzymes largely normal except mild AST elevation, fitting congenital hepatic fibrosis/noncirrhotic portal hypertension phenotype

PMC11256461.diagnosis: Sulfasalazine-induced DRESS syndrome (drug reaction with eosinophilia and systemic symptoms)
rationale_summary: Fever, diffuse blanching morbilliform rash, and tender cervical lymphadenopathy beginning ~3 weeks after starting sulfasalazine and improving only after withdrawal

Systemic involvement consistent with DRESS: hepatitis (ALT 224, AST 78, INR 1.4, hypoalbuminemia), serositis/effusions (pericardial effusion, pleural effusion, massive ascites), hepatosplenomegaly, hypoxia, facial/pharyngeal edema
Hematologic abnormalities with evolving leukocytosis and eosinophilia (up to 11%) and markedly elevated ferritin

Broad infectious and other causes were evaluated/partly excluded; case explicitly met all seven RegiSCAR criteria, and extensive hypermetabolic lymphadenopathy on CT/PET can occur in DRESS and mimic lymphoma

PMC9950348.diagnosis: Aggressive non-Hodgkin lymphoma (e.g., diffuse large B‑cell lymphoma) presenting as a cecal mass with spontaneous tumor lysis syndrome
rationale_summary: Cecal mass with disseminated involvement (omental infiltration, hepatic lesions, ascites, bilateral pleural effusions) suggests systemic malignancy beyond typical localized colon adenocarcinoma
Markedly elevated serum LDH (9900 U/L) is characteristic of high-grade hematologic malignancy
Metabolic pattern meeting Cairo-Bishop TLS criteria (acute kidney injury with hyperuricemia 18.1 mg/dL, hyperkalemia, hyperphosphatemia) is more typical of aggressive lymphoma than solid tumors
Hypercalcemia and very high pleural fluid LDH (15,930 U/L) are atypical for solid-tumor TLS but can occur with lymphoma

PMC9566503.diagnosis: Right ventricular myxoma causing severe right ventricular outflow tract obstruction
rationale_summary: Large (3.5×4.0 cm), rounded lobulated intracardiac mass with clear margins and inhomogeneous content
Pedunculated attachment to the infundibular septum and occupying the RV outflow tract
Produces severe RVOT obstruction (peak gradient 80 mmHg) with RA/RV dilation and severe RV systolic dysfunction
CT shows a heterogeneously enhancing soft-tissue mass in the RVOT without pulmonary embolism, supporting a primary cardiac tumor rather than thromboembolism or infection

PMC5184841.diagnosis: Early repolarization syndrome (J-wave syndrome) causing idiopathic ventricular fibrillation
rationale_summary: Syncope with documented polymorphic VT degenerating to ventricular fibrillation requiring repeated defibrillation
Baseline ECG showed widespread J waves with normal QT interval
Frequent monomorphic PVCs triggered the malignant ventricular arrhythmias; PVC frequency and J-wave amplitude decreased with labetalol/electrolyte repletion
No structural heart disease on echocardiography/cardiac MRI and no metabolic, drug, or electrolyte abnormalities; stress test did not provoke arrhythmia

PMC3920376.diagnosis: Altitude-related expansion of a giant left apical pulmonary bulla (pulmonary barotrauma causing in-flight pleuritic chest pain with transient ipsilateral arm weakness)
rationale_summary: Recurrent, highly predictable episodes occurred only as the aircraft reached cruising altitude and fully resolved on descent
Symptoms were pleuritic left-sided chest pain radiating to the back with consistent left arm weakness during flights
CT pulmonary angiography showed a large solitary left apical bulla (10×8×8 cm) with subtle reduced left upper-zone vascular markings on chest radiograph
Baseline exam, oxygen saturation, pulmonary function tests, and head MRI were normal, supporting a pressure-related mechanical cause rather than chronic cardiopulmonary or neurologic disease

PMC8521291.diagnosis: Functional tic-like disorder (functional neurological symptom disorder with abnormal movements/vocalizations)
rationale_summary: Abrupt adult-onset (age 25) involuntary motor tics and vocalizations over 3 days, which is atypical for Tourette syndrome (usually childhood onset and chronic)
Normal neurologic examination aside from observed tics, without other focal deficits
Temporal association with significant psychosocial stressors and anxiety symptoms (marital separation, returning to daughter's room, impending court date; GAD-7 = 15)
Possible symptom modeling/triggering given prior exposure to a coworker with Tourette's syndrome

PMC10876468.diagnosis: Primary dural lymphoma (dural-based CNS lymphoma) of the tentorium
rationale_summary: Extra-axial mass with broad attachment to the medial tentorium compressing the dorsal brainstem and causing hydrocephalus
Hyperdense on noncontrast CT and shows marked diffusion restriction (DWI hyperintensity with ADC hypointensity), supporting hypercellular tumor such as lymphoma
Intense gadolinium enhancement with extensive perilesional edema and rapid clinical/radiologic progression over ~10 days
Cerebral angiography shows no obvious feeding vessels or tumor blush, arguing against typical vascular meningioma/hemangiopericytoma

PMC6935604.diagnosis: Takotsubo (stress-induced) cardiomyopathy precipitated during initiation of EPOCH chemotherapy
rationale_summary: Abrupt onset (10 hours into chemo infusion) of acute hypoxic respiratory failure with pulmonary edema and cardiogenic shock after previously normal baseline echocardiogram (EF 65%)
Bedside echocardiogram showed classic apical ballooning with severe regional wall-motion abnormalities and markedly reduced EF (~20%)
ECG with new nonspecific T-wave inversions and only modest troponin elevation (peak 0.7 ng/mL) with very high NT-proBNP (7000 pg/mL), supporting stress cardiomyopathy rather than large acute MI
No prior cardiac disease history and presentation occurred in the setting of major physiologic stress (new ATLL with tumor lysis syndrome and chemotherapy initiation)

PMC10682226.diagnosis: Well-differentiated cutaneous squamous cell carcinoma (facial SCC)
rationale_summary: Progressive destructive facial plaques/ulceration/crusting over 3 years with no response to multiple antimicrobial, antimonial, antituberculous, or corticosteroid therapies
Nasal skin biopsy showed epidermal hyperplasia with cellular atypia, increased mitoses, invasive squamous nests, and keratin pearls-diagnostic of well-differentiated SCC
Upper lip biopsy showed actinic cheilitis (premalignant sun-damage change) supporting SCC in this distribution
Staging showed no metastases; infectious workup repeatedly negative (stains/cultures/PCR), making infection less likely despite secondary S. aureus growth

PMC3113998.diagnosis: Severe hypercapnia-induced intracranial hypertension/cerebral edema secondary to acute upper-airway angioedema
rationale_summary: Sudden bilateral facial and tongue swelling with extensive laryngeal edema required emergent intubation for airway protection
Arterial blood gas on controlled ventilation showed marked hypercapnic acidosis (pH 7.22, pCO2 85 mmHg) with adequate oxygenation (pO2 109 mmHg)

Neurologic signs (unresponsiveness, fixed dilated right pupil, blurred disc margins) plus head CT showing effaced sulci, small ventricles, and narrowed basilar cisterns indicate increased intracranial pressure consistent with CO2-related cerebral edema/impending herniation

rationale_summary: 9-month relapsing abdominal pain with persistent incomplete intestinal obstruction and decreased bowel movements
Imaging showed multiple air-fluid levels and segmental small-bowel dilatation with wall enhancement
Enteroscopy demonstrated a small-intestinal ulcer with luminal stenosis (ulcerative stricturing small-bowel disease)
Inflammatory markers (ESR, CRP, procalcitonin) were normal and infection/malignancy were excluded
High-dose steroids/cyclophosphamide improved fever/leukopenia but not the obstructive symptoms, arguing against purely SLE-related mesenteric vasculitis/lupus enteritis
